contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories.", 9: "dateModified": "2026-01-19T02:09:57.563Z", 10: "inLanguage": "en-US" 11: } 12: </script> 13: 14: 15: This directory contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories. 16: 17: ## Directory Structure 18: 19: ``` 20: .ast-grep/ 21: ├── sgconfig.yml # Master configuration 22: ├── duplicate-error-handling.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 23: ├── fs-operations.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 24: └── rules/ 25: ├── api/ # API and HTTP patterns 26: │ ├── auth-checks.yml 27: │ ├── error-responses.yml 28: │ ├── express-route-handlers.yml 29: │ └── request-validation.yml 30: ├── async/ # Asynchronous patterns 31: │ ├── await-patterns.yml 32: │ └── promise-chains.yml 33: ├── config/ # Configuration patterns 34: │ ├── config-objects.yml 35: │ └── env-variables.yml 36: ├── database/ # Database operation patterns 37: │ ├── connection-handling.yml 38: │ ├── prisma-operations.yml 39: │ └── query-builders.yml 40: ├── logging/ # Logging patterns 41: │ ├── console-statements.yml 42: │ └── logger-patterns.yml 43: └── utilities/ # Utility function patterns 44: ├── array-map-filter.yml 45: ├── object-manipulation.yml 46: ├── string-manipulation.yml 47: ├── type-checking.yml 48: └── validation.yml 49: ``` 50: 51: **Total Rules:** 18 active rules across 6 categories 52: 53: ## Rule Categories 54: 55: ### 1. Utilities (5 rules) 56: 57: **Purpose:** Detect common utility operations that could be consolidated 58: 59: - **array-map-filter.yml** - Array iteration methods (map, filter, reduce, forEach, find, findIndex) 60: - **object-manipulation.yml** - Object operations (keys, values, entries, assign, spread, JSON) 61: - **string-manipulation.yml** - String operations (trim, case, split, replace, slice, substring) 62: - **type-checking.yml** - Type checking patterns (typeof, instanceof, Array.isArray) 63: - **validation.yml** - Validation logic (null checks, error throws, early returns) 64: 65: ### 2. API Patterns (4 rules) 66: 67: **Purpose:** Identify API-related patterns for standardization 68: 69: - **express-route-handlers.yml** - Route handler patterns (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) 70: - **auth-checks.yml** - Authentication/authorization patterns (401/403 responses, token checks) 71: - **error-responses.yml** - API error response patterns (status codes, error objects) 72: - **request-validation.yml** - Request validation patterns (body, params, query validation) 73: 74: ### 3. Database (3 rules) 75: 76: **Purpose:** Track database access patterns for repository pattern consolidation 77: 78: - **prisma-operations.yml** - Prisma ORM patterns (findUnique, create, update, delete, etc.) 79: - **query-builders.yml** - Raw SQL query patterns (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) 80: - **connection-handling.yml** - Connection management (connect, disconnect, pooling) 81: 82: ### 4. Configuration (2 rules) 83: 84: **Purpose:** Find configuration access that should be centralized 85: 86: - **env-variables.yml** - Direct process.env access (should use config module) 87: - **config-objects.yml** - Configuration object patterns 88: 89: ### 5. Async Patterns (2 rules) 90: 91: **Purpose:** Detect asynchronous code patterns for consistency 92: 93: - **await-patterns.yml** - Async/await with try-catch patterns 94: - **promise-chains.yml** - Promise chains (.then, .catch, Promise.all, etc.) 95: 96: ### 6. Logging (2 rules) 97: 98: **Purpose:** Track logging patterns for standardization 99: 100: - **console-statements.yml** - Console.log usage (should be replaced with structured logger) 101: - **logger-patterns.yml** - Structured logger usage patterns 102: 103: ## Usage 104: 105: ### Scan a Single Directory 106: 107: ```bash 108: # Scan with all rules 109: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 110: 111: # Scan with specific rule 112: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/config/env-variables.yml sidequest/ 113: 114: # Scan multiple directories 115: ast-grep scan sidequest/ sidequest/output/condense/ directory-scan-reports/ 116: ``` 117: 118: ### Scan by Category 119: 120: ```bash 121: # Scan for all utility patterns 122: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/utilities/ sidequest/ 123: 124: # Scan for all API patterns 125: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/api/ sidequest/ 126: 127: # Scan for all database patterns 128: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/database/ sidequest/ 129: ``` 130: 131: ### Output Formats 132: 133: ```bash 134: # JSON output (for programmatic processing) 135: ast-grep scan --json sidequest/ 136: 137: # Default pretty output 138: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 139: 140: # Count matches only 141: ast-grep scan sidequest/ | grep -c "pattern" 142: ``` 143: 144: ## Test Results (on sidequest/ directory) 145: 146: | Rule Category | Matches Found | Notes | 147: |---------------|---------------|-------| 148: | Logger patterns | 3,405 | Very high usage of structured logging ✅ | 149: | Object manipulation | ~50+ | JSON.stringify, Object.entries common | 150: | Env variables | ~20+ | Some direct process.env access found | 151: | Async/await | ~15+ | Good try-catch error handling patterns | 152: | Array operations | TBD | Need to test | 153: | File system ops | ~20+ | Many fs.readFile, fs.writeFile patterns | 154: 155: ## Integration with Duplicate Detection Pipeline 156: 157: ### Phase 1: Pattern Discovery 158: 1. Run all rules across target repositories 159: 2. Collect matches with file locations and line numbers 160: 3. Group by pattern type 161: 162: ### Phase 2: Similarity Analysis 163: 1. Extract AST nodes for each match 164: 2. Compare structural similarity 165: 3. Calculate consolidation scores 166: 167: ### Phase 3: Recommendations 168: 1. Generate consolidation suggestions 169: 2. Prioritize by frequency and impact 170: 3. Suggest abstraction tier (local util, shared package, MCP, agent) 171: 172: ## Adding New Rules 173: 174: ### Rule Template 175: 176: ```yaml 177: id: rule-name 178: language: javascript # or typescript, python, etc. 179: rule: 180: any: # or 'all', 'pattern', 'not', etc. 181: - pattern: $PATTERN1 182: - pattern: $PATTERN2 183: message: Description of what was found 184: severity: info # or warning, error 185: note: Additional context for consolidation analysis 186: ``` 187: 188: ### Best Practices 189: 190: 1. **Use descriptive IDs:** `category-specific-name` (e.g., `database-prisma-operations`) 191: 2. **Provide context:** Include helpful notes about consolidation potential 192: 3. **Test thoroughly:** Verify rule matches intended patterns without false positives 193: 4. **Use appropriate severity:** 194: - `info` - Pattern tracking for analysis 195: - `warning` - Anti-pattern or should be refactored 196: - `error` - Code that must be changed 197: 5. **Document expected matches:** Add examples in rule comments 198: 199: ### Testing New Rules 200: 201: ```bash 202: # Test rule syntax 203: ast-grep test .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml 204: 205: # Test against sample code 206: echo 'const x = process.env.FOO' | ast-grep -p 'process.env.$VAR' 207: 208: # Scan specific directory 209: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml path/to/test/ 210: ``` 211: 212: ## Pattern Syntax Reference 213: 214: ### Meta Variables 215: 216: - `$VAR` - Matches a single AST node 217: - `$$$` - Matches zero or more nodes 218: - `$$$ARGS` - Named multi-node match 219: - `$_VAR` - Non-capturing (performance optimization) 220: 221: ### Matchers 222: 223: ```yaml 224: rule: 225: pattern: $EXACT_MATCH # Single pattern 226: any: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match any pattern (OR) 227: all: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match all patterns (AND) 228: not: { pattern: $PAT } # Negation 229: inside: { pattern: $CONTEXT } # Must be inside context 230: ``` 231: 232: ### Advanced Patterns 233: 234: ```yaml 235: # Match repeated variables (detect duplicates) 236: pattern: $A == $A 237: 238: # Match multi-line code blocks 239: pattern: | 240: if ($COND) { 241: $$$BODY 242: } 243: 244: # Match specific structures 245: pattern: | 246: class $CLASS { 247: $METHOD($$$ARGS) { 248: $$$BODY 249: } 250: } 251: ``` 252: 253: ## Next Steps 254: 255: 1. **Expand rule library:** 256: - React/Vue component patterns 257: - Test framework patterns 258: - Module import/export patterns 259: 260: 2. **Create test suite:** 261: - Sample code for each pattern 262: - Expected match counts 263: - Automated rule validation 264: 265: 3. **Build aggregation tool:** 266: - Collect all scan results 267: - Deduplicate matches 268: - Generate consolidated reports 269: 270: 4. **Integrate with pydantic:** 271: - Define data models for matches 272: - Structure results for analysis 273: - Enable pipeline processing 274: 275: ## Resources 276: 277: - [ast-grep Documentation](https://ast-grep.github.io/) 278: - [Pattern Syntax Guide](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/pattern-syntax.html) 279: - name: Verify setup 47: run: pnpm run verify 48: - name: Run TypeScript type checking 49: run: pnpm run typecheck 50: - name: Run unit tests 51: run: pnpm test 52: - name: Run integration tests 53: env: 54: NODE_ENV: test 55: CI: true 56: run: pnpm run test:integration 57: continue-on-error: true 58: - name: Check dashboard files exist 59: run: | 60: if [ -d public ]; then 61: test -f public/index.html && test -f public/dashboard.css && test -f public/dashboard.js 62: echo "✅ Dashboard files verified (public/)" 63: elif [ -d frontend ]; then 64: echo "✅ Dashboard uses frontend/ build (validated separately)" 65: else 66: echo "⚠️ No dashboard directory found (skipping)" 67: fi 68: - name: Test Python pipeline integration 69: run: | 70: echo "Testing Python pipeline stages (3-7)..." 71: python3 << 'PYEOF' 72: import sys 73: sys.path.insert(0, 'sidequest/pipeline-core') 74: from models.code_block import CodeBlock, SourceLocation 75: from models.duplicate_group import DuplicateGroup 76: print('✅ Pydantic models imported successfully') 77: PYEOF 78: python3 << 'PYEOF' 79: import sys 80: sys.path.insert(0, 'sidequest/pipeline-core') 81: from similarity.structural import calculate_structural_similarity 82: score, method = calculate_structural_similarity('function a() { return 1; }', 'function b() { return 1; }') 83: assert 0 <= score <= 1, f'Invalid score: {score}' 84: print(f'✅ Structural similarity working (score: {score:.2f}, method: {method})') 85: PYEOF 86: echo "✅ Python pipeline integration verified" 87: lint: 88: name: Lint and Code Quality 89: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 90: steps: 91: - name: Checkout code 117: uses: actions/checkout@v4 118: - name: Set up pnpm 119: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 120: with: 121: version: 9 122: - name: Set up Node.js 123: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 124: with: 125: node-version: '24.x' 126: cache: 'pnpm' 127: - name: Install dependencies 128: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 129: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 130: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 131: continue-on-error: true 132: - name: Validate static assets 133: run: | 134: if [ -d public ]; then 135: grep -q "AlephAuto Dashboard" public/index.html || (echo "❌ Dashboard title not found" && exit 1) 136: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.css 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.css) 137: if [ $SIZE -lt 1000 ]; then 138: echo "❌ CSS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 139: exit 1 140: fi 141: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.js 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.js) 142: if [ $SIZE -lt 5000 ]; then 143: echo "❌ JS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 144: exit 1 145: fi 146: echo "✅ Static assets validated (public/)" 147: elif [ -d frontend ]; then 148: echo "✅ Dashboard uses frontend/ (build validated via pnpm run build:frontend)" 149: else 150: echo "⚠️ No dashboard directory found (skipping static asset validation)" 151: fi 152: - name: Test API server starts 153: run: | 154: timeout 30 node --strip-types api/server.ts & 155: SERVER_PID=$! 156: echo "Waiting for server to start..." 157: for i in {1..15}; do 158: if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then 159: echo "✅ Server ready after ${i} seconds" 160: break 161: fi 162: if [ $i -eq 15 ]; then 163: echo "❌ Server failed to start within 15 seconds" 164: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 165: exit 1 166: fi 167: sleep 1 168: done 169: curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || (echo "❌ Health check failed" && exit 1) 170: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 171: echo "✅ API server validated" 172: env: 173: NODE_ENV: test 174: JOBS_API_PORT: 8080 1: name: CD - Production Deployment 2: on: 3: workflow_dispatch: 4: jobs: 5: deploy: 6: name: Deploy to Production 7: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 8: environment: production 9: steps: 10: - name: Checkout code 11: uses: actions/checkout@v4 12: - name: Set up pnpm 13: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 14: with: 15: version: 9 16: - name: Set up Node.js 17: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 18: with: 19: node-version: '20.x' 20: cache: 'pnpm' 21: - name: Set up Python 22: uses: actions/setup-python@v5 23: with: 24: python-version: '3.11' 25: cache: 'pip' 26: - name: Install Node.js dependencies 27: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 28: - name: Install Python dependencies 29: run: | 30: python -m pip install --upgrade pip 31: pip install -r requirements.txt 32: - name: Install Doppler CLI 33: run: | 34: curl -sLf --retry 3 --tlsv1.2 --proto "=https" 'https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/gpg.DE2A7741.key' | sudo apt-key add - 35: echo "deb https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/deb/debian any-version main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/doppler-cli.list 36: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install doppler 37: - name: Install PM2 globally 38: run: pnpm add -g pm2 39: - name: Validate deployment files 40: run: | 41: echo "Validating deployment artifacts..." 42: test -f api/server.ts || test -f api/server.js || (echo "❌ API server not found" && exit 1) 43: grep -q '"dashboard"' package.json || (echo "❌ Dashboard script not found" && exit 1) 44: echo "✅ All deployment files validated" 45: - name: Configure Doppler 46: run: | 47: echo "${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }}" | doppler configure set token --scope / --silent 48: env: 49: DOPPLER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }} 50: - name: Deploy with rsync 51: uses: burnett01/rsync-deployments@6.0.0 52: with: 53: switches: -avzr --delete --exclude='.git*' --exclude='node_modules' --exclude='venv' --exclude='tests' 54: path: ./ 55: remote_path: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 56: remote_host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 57: remote_user: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 58: remote_key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 59: - name: Install dependencies on server 60: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 61: with: 62: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 63: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 64: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 65: script: | 66: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 67: mkdir -p logs 68: command -v pnpm || npm install -g pnpm 69: doppler run -c prd -- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 70: python3 -m venv venv 71: source venv/bin/activate 72: pip install --upgrade pip 73: pip install -r requirements.txt 74: - name: Restart services with PM2 75: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 76: with: 77: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 78: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 79: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 80: script: | 81: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 82: if pm2 list | grep -q "aleph-dashboard\|aleph-worker"; then 83: echo "Services found, restarting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 84: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 restart config/ecosystem.config.cjs 85: else 86: echo "Services not found, starting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 87: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 start config/ecosystem.config.cjs 88: fi 89: pm2 save 90: pm2 status 91: - name: Health check 92: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 93: with: 94: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 95: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 96: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 97: script: | 98: echo "Waiting for services to start..." 99: sleep 10 100: if redis-cli ping | grep -q "PONG"; then 101: echo "✅ Redis is healthy" 102: else 103: echo "⚠️ Redis not available (caching disabled)" 104: fi 105: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/health) 106: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 107: echo "✅ Dashboard is healthy" 108: else 109: echo "❌ Dashboard health check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 110: exit 1 111: fi 112: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/api/health/doppler) 113: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 114: echo "✅ Doppler health monitor is active" 115: else 116: echo "❌ Doppler health monitor check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 117: exit 1 118: fi 119: PIPELINE_COUNT=$(curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/status | jq '.pipelines | length') 120: if [ "$PIPELINE_COUNT" -ge 7 ]; then 121: echo "✅ Dashboard returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines (E1 verified)" 122: else 123: echo "❌ Dashboard only returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines, expected >= 7" 124: exit 1 125: fi 126: REPOMIX_RESPONSE=$(curl -s 'http://localhost:8080/api/sidequest/pipeline-runners/repomix/jobs?limit=10') 127: REPOMIX_STATUS=$(echo $REPOMIX_RESPONSE | jq -r '.total // "skip"') 128: if [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "skip" ] && [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "null" ]; then 129: echo "✅ Pagination endpoint working (E2 verified)" 130: else 131: echo "⚠️ Pagination endpoint returned no data (may be normal for new deployment)" 132: fi 133: - name: Notify deployment success 134: if: success() 135: run: | 136: echo "🚀 Deployment successful!" 137: echo "📊 Dashboard: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/" 138: echo "❤️ Health: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/health" 139: - name: Rollback on failure 140: if: failure() 141: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 142: with: 143: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 144: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 145: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 146: script: | 147: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 148: echo "⚠️ Deployment failed, attempting rollback..." 149: pm2 resurrect 150: echo "Rollback complete. if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { 59: e.preventDefault(); 60: handleItemClick(activityId); 61: } 62: }; 63: const handleClearClick = () => { 64: if (onClear) { 65: onClear(); 66: } 67: }; 68: return ( 69: <div className="activity-feed"> 70: {} 71: <div className="activity-feed-header"> 72: <h2 className="activity-feed-title"> 73: <span className="title-icon" aria-hidden="true">⚡</span> 74: 56: this.send({ 57: type: 'subscribe', 58: channels: ['jobs', 'pipelines', 'system', 'activity', 'scans', '*'] 59: }); 60: break; 61: case 'subscribed': 62: console.log('[WebSocket] Subscribed to channels:', message.channels); 63: break; 64: case 'pong': 65: break; 66: case 'job:created': 67: this.handleJobCreated(message.data); 68: break; 69: case 'job:started': 70: this.handleJobStarted(message.data); 71: break; 72: case 'job:progress': 73: this.handleJobProgress(message.data); 74: break; 75: case 'job:completed': 76: this.handleJobCompleted(message.data); 77: break; 78: case 'job:failed': 79: this.handleJobFailed(message.data); 80: break; 81: case 'job:cancelled': 82: this.handleJobCancelled(message.data); 83: break; 84: case 'pipeline:status': 85: this.handlePipelineStatus(message.data); 86: break; 87: case 'system:status': 88: useDashboardStore.getState().setSystemStatus(message.data); 89: break; 90: case 'activity:new': 91: console.log('[WebSocket] New activity:', message.activity); 92: if (message.activity) { 93: this.handleActivityEvent(message.activity); 94: } 95: break; 96: case 'error': 97: console.error('[WebSocket] Server error:', message.error); 98: break; 99: default: 100: console.log('[WebSocket] Unknown message type:', message.type); 188: } 189: private handleJobFailed(job: Job): void { 190: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 191: store.setActiveJobs(store.activeJobs.filter((j) => j.id !== job.id)); 192: store.updatePipeline(job.pipelineId, { 193: status: 'failed' as any, 194: lastError: job.error, 195: }); 196: this.addActivity({ 197: id: `activity-${job.id}-failed`, 198: type: 'failed' as ActivityType, 199: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 200: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 201: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... failed: ${job.error || 'Unknown error'}`, 202: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 203: jobId: job.id, 204: severity: 'error', 205: details: { 206: error: job.error, 207: errorType: job.errorType, 208: }, 209: }); 210: } 211: private handleJobCancelled(job: Job): void { 212: const store = useDashboardStore.getState(); 213: store.removeJob(job.id); 214: this.addActivity({ 215: id: `activity-${job.id}-cancelled`, 216: type: 'cancelled' as ActivityType, 217: pipelineId: job.pipelineId, 218: pipelineName: job.pipelineName || 'Unknown Pipeline', 219: message: `Job ${job.id.substring(0, 8)}... cancelled`, 220: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 221: jobId: job.id, 222: severity: 'warning', 223: }); 224: } 225: private handlePipelineStatus(pipeline: Pipeline): void { 226: useDashboardStore.getState().updatePipeline(pipeline.id, pipeline); 227: } 228: private handleActivityEvent(activity: any): void { 229: const activityType = ACTIVITY_TYPE_MAP[activity.type] ?? 'https://schema.org/Event', 244: ...activity, 245: }); 246: } 247: send(message: any): void { 248: if (this.socket?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) { 249: this.socket.send(JSON.stringify(message)); 250: } 251: } 252: disconnect(): void { 253: this.stopHeartbeat(); 254: if (this.socket) { 255: this.socket.close(); 256: this.socket = null; 257: } 258: } 259: isConnected(): boolean { 260: return └── package.json # Dependencies and scripts 36: ``` 37: 38: ## Getting Started 39: 40: ### Prerequisites 41: 42: - Node.js >= 18.0.0 43: - npm or yarn 44: 45: ### Installation 46: 47: ```bash 48: cd frontend 49: npm install 50: ``` 51: 52: ### Development 53: 54: Start the development server: 55: 56: ```bash 57: npm run dev 58: ``` 59: 60: The dashboard will be available at `http://localhost:5173` 61: 62: The Vite dev server is configured to proxy API requests: 63: - `/api/*` → `http://localhost:8080` 64: - `/ws` (WebSocket) → `ws://localhost:8080` 65: 66: ### Build 67: 68: Create a production build: 69: 70: ```bash 71: npm run build 72: ``` 73: 74: Preview the production build: 75: 76: ```bash 77: npm run preview 78: ``` 79: 80: ### Type Checking 81: 82: Run TypeScript type checking without emitting files: 83: 84: ```bash 85: npm run typecheck 86: ``` 87: 88: ## Environment Variables 89: 90: Create a `.env.local` file for local development (optional): 91: 92: ```env 93: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 94: VITE_WS_URL=ws://localhost:8080 95: ``` 96: 97: ## Implementation Status 98: 99: ### ✅ Completed 100: 101: - [x] Project setup and configuration specification 162: - `docs/DASHBOARD_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md` - Technical implementation guide 163: - `docs/DASHBOARD_VISUAL_MOCKUPS.md` - Design tokens and mockups 164: - `docs/DASHBOARD_QUICK_REFERENCE.md` - Developer cheat sheet 165: 166: ## API Integration 167: 168: The dashboard connects to the AlephAuto backend API running on port 8080: 169: 170: **Health Check** 171: - `GET /health` - System health status 172: 173: **Pipelines** 174: - `GET /api/pipelines` - List all pipelines 175: - `GET /api/pipelines/:id` - Get pipeline details 176: 177: **Jobs** 178: - `GET /api/jobs?status=running` - Get active jobs 179: - `GET /api/jobs?status=queued` - Get queued jobs 180: - `GET /api/jobs/:id` - Get job details 181: - `GET /api/jobs/:id/logs` - Get job logs 182: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/cancel` - Cancel a job 183: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/retry` - Retry a failed job 184: 185: **Scans** 186: - `POST /api/scan` - Trigger a new scan 187: - `GET /api/scan/:id` - Get scan results 188: 189: ## Contributing 190: 191: When adding new features: 192: 1. Follow the existing project structure 193: 2. Add TypeScript types in `src/types/` 194: 3. Use Zustand for state management 195: 4. Follow Tailwind CSS utility patterns 196: 5. 68: async function scanDir(dir) { 69: const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true }); 70: for (const entry of entries) { 71: const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name); 72: if (entry.isDirectory()) { 73: await scanDir(fullPath); 74: } else if (entry.name.endsWith('.error.json')) { 75: const age = await getFileAgeDays(fullPath); 76: const size = (await fs.stat(fullPath)).size; 77: errorLogs.push({ 78: path: fullPath, 79: name: entry.name, 80: ageDays: age, 81: sizeBytes: size, 82: directory: path.relative(baseDir, dir) 83: }); 84: } 85: } 86: } 87: await scanDir(baseDir); 88: return errorLogs; 89: } 90: async function scanArchivedLogs(archiveDir) { 91: try { 92: await fs.access(archiveDir); 93: } catch { 94: return []; 95: } 96: const archivedLogs = []; 97: const entries = await fs.readdir(archiveDir, { withFileTypes: true }); 98: for (const entry of entries) { 99: if (entry.name.endsWith('.gz')) { 100: const fullPath = path.join(archiveDir, entry.name); 101: const age = await getFileAgeDays(fullPath); 102: const size = (await fs.stat(fullPath)).size; 103: archivedLogs.push({ 104: path: fullPath, 105: name: entry.name, 106: ageDays: age, 107: sizeBytes: size 108: }); 109: } 110: } 111: return archivedLogs; 112: } 113: function formatBytes(bytes) { 114: if (bytes === 0) return '0 B'; 115: const k = BYTES_PER_KB; 116: const sizes = ['B', 'KB', 'MB', 'GB']; 117: const i = Math.floor(Math.log(bytes) / Math.log(k)); 134: const archiveName = `${path.basename(log.name, '.json')}-${timestamp}.json.gz`; 135: const archivePath = path.join(archiveDir, archiveName); 136: if (dryRun) { 137: if (verbose) { 138: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would archive: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 139: } 140: } else { 141: try { 142: await compressFile(log.path, archivePath); 143: await fs.unlink(log.path); 144: if (verbose) { 145: console.log(` ✓ Archived: ${log.name} → ${archiveName}`); 146: } 147: archivedCount++; '[DRY RUN] Would delete' : 'Deleted'; 185: console.log(`${action} ${toDelete.length} old archives (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 186: return { deleted: deletedCount, totalSize }; 187: } 188: function printCleanupSummary(archiveResult: { archived: number; totalSize: number }, deleteResult: { deleted: number; totalSize: number }, dryRun: boolean) { 189: console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(50)); 190: console.log('📊 Summary'); 191: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 192: console.log(`Error logs archived: ${archiveResult.archived}`); 193: console.log(`Old archives deleted: ${deleteResult.deleted}`); 194: console.log(`Total space reclaimed: ${formatBytes(archiveResult.totalSize + deleteResult.totalSize)}`); 195: if (dryRun) console.log('\n⚠️ This was a dry run - no files were modified'); 196: } 197: async function cleanup(options) { 198: console.log('\n🧹 Error Log Cleanup\n'); 199: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 200: console.log(`Retention period: ${options.retentionDays} days`); 201: console.log(`Archive retention: ${ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS} days`); 202: console.log(`Mode: ${options.dryRun ? 210: console.log(` Old (>${options.retentionDays} days): ${errorLogs.length - recent}\n`); 211: } 212: const archiveResult = await archiveOldLogs(errorLogs, options.retentionDays, options.dryRun, options.verbose); 213: const archiveDir = path.join(LOGS_BASE_DIR, 'archive'); 214: const archivedLogs = await scanArchivedLogs(archiveDir); 215: if (archivedLogs.length > 0) { 216: console.log(`\n📦 Found ${archivedLogs.length} archived logs`); class SemanticAnnotator: 220: def __init__(self, collect_timing: bool = False) -> None: 221: self.collect_timing = collect_timing 222: self.timing: dict[str, TimingMetrics] = {} 223: if collect_timing: 224: try: 225: from ..utils.timing import TimingMetrics as TM 226: except ImportError: 227: from utils.timing import TimingMetrics as TM 228: self.timing = { 229: "operations": TM("operations"), 230: "domains": TM("domains"), 231: "patterns": TM("patterns"), 232: "data_types": TM("data_types"), 233: "intent": TM("intent"), 234: } 235: def get_timing_report(self) -> dict[str, dict]: 236: return {name: m.to_dict() for name, m in self.timing.items() if m.count > 0} 237: def extract_annotation(self, block: CodeBlock) -> SemanticAnnotation: 238: code = block.source_code 239: tags = block.tags if hasattr(block, "tags") else [] 240: category = block.category if hasattr(block, "category") else "unknown" 241: if hasattr(category, "value"): 242: category = category.value 243: if self.collect_timing: 244: try: 245: from ..utils.timing import Timer 246: except ImportError: 247: from utils.timing import Timer 248: with Timer(self.timing["operations"]): 249: operations = self._extract_operations(code) 250: with Timer(self.timing["domains"]): 251: domains = self._extract_domains(code, tags) 252: with Timer(self.timing["patterns"]): 253: patterns = self._extract_patterns(code) 254: with Timer(self.timing["data_types"]): 255: data_types = self._extract_data_types(code) 256: with Timer(self.timing["intent"]): 257: intent = self._infer_intent(operations, domains, patterns) 258: else: 259: operations = self._extract_operations(code) 260: domains = self._extract_domains(code, tags) 261: patterns = self._extract_patterns(code) 262: data_types = self._extract_data_types(code) 263: intent = self._infer_intent(operations, domains, patterns) 264: return SemanticAnnotation( 265: category=category, 266: operations=operations, 267: domains=domains, 268: patterns=patterns, 269: data_types=data_types, 270: intent=intent, 271: ) 272: def _extract_operations(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 273: operations: set[str] = set() 274: for compiled_pattern, op in _COMPILED_ALL_OPERATIONS: 275: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 276: operations.add(op) 277: return operations 278: def _extract_domains(self, code: str, tags: list[str]) -> set[str]: 279: domains: set[str] = set() 280: text = code + " " + " ".join(tags) 281: for compiled_pattern, domain in _COMPILED_DOMAIN: 282: if compiled_pattern.search(text): 283: domains.add(domain) 284: return domains 285: def _extract_patterns(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 286: patterns: set[str] = set() 287: for compiled_pattern, name in _COMPILED_CODE_PATTERNS: 288: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 289: patterns.add(name) 290: return patterns 291: def _extract_data_types(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 292: data_types: set[str] = set() 293: for compiled_pattern, dtype in _COMPILED_DATA_TYPES: 294: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 295: data_types.add(dtype) 296: return data_types 297: def _infer_intent( 298: self, 299: operations: set[str], 300: domains: set[str], 301: patterns: set[str], 302: ) -> str: 303: parts: list[str] = [] 304: if operations: 305: parts.append("+".join(sorted(operations))) 306: if domains: 307: parts.append("on:" + "+".join(sorted(domains))) 308: if patterns: 309: parts.append("with:" + "+".join(sorted(patterns))) 310: return "|".join(parts) if parts else "unknown" 1: """Type stubs for logStart(logger, 'cached repository scan', { repoPath, options }); 82: const repoStatus = await this.gitTracker.getRepositoryStatus(repoPath); 83: const shouldUseCache = await this._shouldUseCache(repoPath, repoStatus, options); 84: if (shouldUseCache) { 85: const cachedResult = await this._getCachedResult(repoPath, repoStatus.current_commit); 86: if (cachedResult) { 87: from cache'); 94: return { 95: ...cachedResult, 96: scan_metadata: { 97: ...cachedResult.scan_metadata, 98: duration_seconds: duration, 99: git_status: repoStatus 100: } 101: }; 102: } 103: } 104: logger.info({ repoPath, reason: options.forceRefresh ? 'forced_refresh' : 'cache_miss' }, 'Running fresh scan'); 105: const scanResult: ScanResult = await this.scanner.scanRepository(repoPath, options); 106: if (this.cacheEnabled && this.cache && repoStatus.current_commit) { 107: await this._cacheResult(repoPath, repoStatus.current_commit, scanResult); 108: } 109: const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / TIME_MS.SECOND; 110: logger.info({ 111: repoPath, 112: fromCache: false, 113: duration, 114: duplicates: scanResult.metrics?.total_duplicate_groups ?? 0 115: }, 'Scan completed from fresh scan'); 116: return { 117: ...scanResult, 118: scan_metadata: { 119: ...scanResult.scan_metadata, 120: duration_seconds: duration, 121: git_status: repoStatus, 122: from_cache: false 123: } 124: }; 125: } catch (error) { 126: logError(logger, error, 'Cached scan failed', { repoPath }); 127: Sentry.captureException(error, { 128: tags: { 129: component: 'cached-scanner', 130: repository: repoPath 131: } 132: }); 133: throw error; 134: } 135: } 136: private async _shouldUseCache( 137: repoPath: string, 138: repoStatus: RepositoryStatus, 139: options: CachedScanOptions 140: ): Promise<boolean> { 141: if (options.forceRefresh ?? this.forceRefresh) { 142: logSkip(logger, 'cache', 'forced refresh', { repoPath }); 143: return false; 144: } 145: if (!this.cacheEnabled || !this.cache) { 146: logSkip(logger, 'cache', 'caching disabled', { repoPath }); 147: return false; 148: } 149: if (!repoStatus.is_git_repository) { 150: logSkip(logger, 'cache', 'not a git repository', { repoPath }); 151: return false; 152: } 153: if (this.trackUncommitted && repoStatus.has_uncommitted_changes) { 154: logSkip(logger, 'cache', 'has uncommitted changes', { repoPath }); 155: return false; 156: } 157: return true; 158: } 159: private async _getCachedResult( 160: repoPath: string, 161: commitHash: string | null 162: ): Promise<CachedScanResult | null> { 163: try { 164: const cachedResult = await this.cache!.getCachedScan(repoPath, commitHash); 165: if (cachedResult) { 166: const cacheAge = await this.cache!.getCacheAge(repoPath, commitHash); 167: return { 168: ...(cachedResult as unknown as CachedScanResult), 169: cache_metadata: { 170: ...cachedResult.cache_metadata, 171: age: cacheAge, 172: age_hours: cacheAge !== null ? cacheAge / TIME_MS.HOUR : null, 173: age_days: cacheAge !== null ? cacheAge / TIME_MS.DAY : null 174: } 175: }; 176: } 177: return null; 178: } catch (error) { 179: logWarn(logger, error as Error | null, 'Failed to get cached result', { repoPath }); 180: return null; 181: } 182: } 183: private async _cacheResult( 184: repoPath: string, 185: commitHash: string, 186: scanResult: ScanResult 187: ): Promise<void> { 188: try { 189: await this.cache!.cacheScan(repoPath, commitHash, scanResult as unknown as ScanResultWithCache); 190: } catch (error) { 191: logWarn(logger, error as Error | null, 'Failed to cache scan result (non-fatal)', { repoPath }); 192: } 193: } 194: async invalidateCache(repoPath: string): Promise<number> { 195: if (!this.cache) { 196: logWarn(logger, null, 'Cannot invalidate cache: cache not initialized', { repoPath }); 197: return 0; 198: } 199: try { 200: logger.info({ repoPath }, 'Invalidating cache for repository'); 201: const deletedCount = await this.cache.invalidateCache(repoPath); 202: logger.info({ 203: repoPath, 204: entriesDeleted: deletedCount 205: }, 'Cache invalidated successfully'); 206: return deletedCount; 207: } catch (error) { 208: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to invalidate cache', { repoPath }); 209: throw error; 210: } 211: } 212: async getCacheStatus(repoPath: string): Promise<CacheStatusResult> { 213: try { 214: const repoStatus = await this.gitTracker.getRepositoryStatus(repoPath); 215: if (!repoStatus.current_commit) { 216: return { 217: is_cached: false, 218: reason: 'not_a_git_repository', 219: cache_age_ms: null, 220: cache_age_hours: null, 221: cache_age_days: null, 222: metadata: null, 223: repository_status: repoStatus 224: }; 225: } 226: const isCached = await this.cache?.isCached(repoPath, repoStatus.current_commit); 227: const cacheAge = isCached ? await this.cache!.getCacheAge(repoPath, repoStatus.current_commit) : null; 228: const metadata = isCached ? await this.cache!.getCacheMetadata(repoPath, repoStatus.current_commit) : null; 229: return { 230: is_cached: isCached ?? false, 231: cache_age_ms: cacheAge, 232: cache_age_hours: cacheAge != null ? cacheAge / TIME_MS.HOUR : null, 233: cache_age_days: cacheAge != null ? cacheAge / TIME_MS.DAY : null, 234: metadata, 235: repository_status: repoStatus 236: }; 237: } catch (error) { 238: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to get cache status', { repoPath }); 239: throw error; 240: } 241: } 242: async getStats(): Promise<ScannerStats> { 243: const cacheStats = this.cache ? await this.cache.getStats() : null; 244: return { 245: cache_enabled: this.cacheEnabled, 246: force_refresh: this.forceRefresh, 247: track_uncommitted: this.trackUncommitted, 248: cache_initialized: this.cache !== null, 249: cache_stats: cacheStats 250: }; 251: } 252: async warmCache(repoPaths: string[], options: CachedScanOptions = {}): Promise<WarmUpResults> { 253: logStart(logger, 'cache warm-up', { repositoryCount: repoPaths.length }); 254: const results: WarmUpResults = { 255: total: repoPaths.length, 256: successful: 0, 257: failed: 0, 258: errors: [] 259: }; 260: for (const repoPath of repoPaths) { 261: try { 262: await this.scanRepository(repoPath, { 263: ...options, 264: forceRefresh: true 265: }); 266: results.successful++; 267: logger.info({ 268: repoPath, 269: progress: `${results.successful}/${results.total}` 270: }, 'Repository scanned for cache warm-up'); 271: } catch (error) { 272: results.failed++; 273: results.errors.push({ 274: repository: repoPath, 275: error: (error as Error).message 276: }); 277: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to scan repository for cache warm-up', { repoPath }); 278: } 279: } 280: logger.info(results, 'Cache warm-up completed'); 281: return results; 282: } 283: } 1: import { exec } from 'child_process'; 2: import { promisify } from 'util'; 3: import fs from 'fs/promises'; 9: export interface CommitMetadata { 10: hash: string; 11: shortHash: string; 12: author: string; 13: email: string; 14: date: string; 15: message: string; 16: } 17: export interface RepositoryStatus { 18: is_git_repository: boolean; 19: current_commit: string | null; 20: short_commit: string | null; 21: branch: string | null; 22: has_uncommitted_changes: boolean; 23: remote_url: string | null; 24: scanned_at: string; 25: } 26: export class GitCommitTracker { 27: async getRepositoryCommit(repoPath: string): Promise<string | null> { 28: try { 29: const { stdout } = await execPromise('git rev-parse HEAD', { 30: cwd: repoPath 31: }); 32: const commitHash = stdout.trim(); 33: logger.info({ repoPath, commitHash }, 'Retrieved repository commit hash'); 34: return commitHash; 35: } catch (error) { 36: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: (error as Error).message }, 'Not a git repository or git not available'); 37: return null; 38: } 39: } 40: async getShortCommit(repoPath: string): Promise<string | null> { 41: try { 42: const { stdout } = await execPromise('git rev-parse --short HEAD', { 43: cwd: repoPath 44: }); 45: const shortHash = stdout.trim(); 46: logger.debug({ repoPath, shortHash }, 'Retrieved short commit hash'); 47: return shortHash; 48: } catch (error) { 49: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: (error as Error).message }, 'Failed to get short commit hash'); 50: return null; 51: } 52: } 53: async hasChanged(repoPath: string, lastCommit: string | null): Promise<boolean> { 54: if (!lastCommit) { 55: return true; 56: } 57: const currentCommit = await this.getRepositoryCommit(repoPath); 58: if (!currentCommit) { 59: logger.warn({ repoPath }, 'Cannot determine if repository changed (not a git repo)'); 60: return true; 61: } 62: const changed = currentCommit !== lastCommit; 63: logger.info({ 64: repoPath, 65: lastCommit: lastCommit.substring(0, 7), 66: currentCommit: currentCommit.substring(0, 7), 67: changed 68: }, 'Checked repository for changes'); 69: return changed; 70: } 71: async getChangedFiles(repoPath: string, fromCommit: string | null): Promise<string[]> { 72: try { 73: const currentCommit = await this.getRepositoryCommit(repoPath); 74: if (!currentCommit || !fromCommit) { 75: logger.warn({ repoPath }, 'Cannot get changed files (missing commit hash)'); 76: return []; 77: } 78: const { stdout } = await execPromise( 79: `git diff --name-only ${fromCommit} ${currentCommit}`, 80: { cwd: repoPath } 81: ); 82: const changedFiles = stdout 83: .trim() 84: .split('\n') 85: .filter(file => file.length > 0); 86: logger.info({ 87: repoPath, 88: fromCommit: fromCommit.substring(0, 7), 89: toCommit: currentCommit.substring(0, 7), 90: filesChanged: changedFiles.length 91: }, 'Retrieved changed files'); 92: return changedFiles; 93: } catch (error) { 94: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Failed to get changed files'); 95: return []; 96: } 97: } 98: async getCommitMetadata(repoPath: string, commitHash = 'HEAD'): Promise<CommitMetadata | null> { 99: try { 100: const { stdout } = await execPromise( 101: `git show -s --format='%H|%an|%ae|%at|%s' ${commitHash}`, 102: { cwd: repoPath } 103: ); 254: ) 255: return True 256: def _try_add_by_location( 257: block: CodeBlock, 258: seen_locations: set[str], 259: unique_blocks: list[CodeBlock], 260: ) -> None: 261: """Add block using location-based deduplication.""" 262: location_key = f"{block.location.file_path}:{block.location.line_start}" 263: if location_key not in seen_locations: 264: seen_locations.add(location_key) 265: unique_blocks.append(block) 266: def deduplicate_blocks(blocks: list[CodeBlock]) -> list[CodeBlock]: 267: """Remove duplicate code blocks from the same location and function. 268: Deduplicate Pattern Matches (priority four) 269: ast-grep patterns can match the same code multiple times within a function. 270: This removes duplicates based on file:function_name, keeping only the earliest match. 271: """ 272: seen_locations: set[str] = set() 273: seen_functions: dict[str, CodeBlock] = {} 274: unique_blocks: list[CodeBlock] = [] 275: for i, block in enumerate(blocks): 276: function_name = _get_function_name_from_tags(block.tags) 277: if i < BlockExtraction.DEBUG_LOG_DEDUP_LIMIT: 278: _debug( 279: f"dedup block {i}: {block.location.file_path}:{block.location.line_start}, func={function_name}" 280: ) 281: if function_name: 282: _try_add_by_function(block, function_name, seen_functions, unique_blocks) 283: else: 284: _try_add_by_location(block, seen_locations, unique_blocks) 285: removed = len(blocks) - len(unique_blocks) 286: if removed > 0: 287: sys.stderr.write( 288: f"Deduplication: Removed {removed} duplicate blocks ({len(seen_functions)} unique functions, {len(seen_locations)} unique locations)\n" 289: ) 290: return unique_blocks 291: def _create_code_block(match: Dict, repository_info: Dict) -> CodeBlock: 292: block_key = f"{match['file_path']}:{match['line_start']}" 293: block_hash = hashlib.sha256(block_key.encode()).hexdigest()[ 294: : BlockExtraction.BLOCK_HASH_LENGTH 295: ] 296: block_id = f"cb_{block_hash}" 297: # Map pattern_id to category 298: category = PATTERN_CATEGORY_MAP.get(match["rule_id"], "utility") 299: source_code = match.get("matched_text", "") 300: function_name = extract_function_name( 301: source_code, 302: file_path=match["file_path"], 303: line_start=match["line_start"], 304: repo_path=repository_info["path"], 305: ) 306: return CodeBlock( 307: block_id=block_id, 308: pattern_id=match["rule_id"], 309: location=SourceLocation( 310: file_path=match["file_path"], 311: line_start=match["line_start"], 312: line_end=match.get("line_end", match["line_start"]), 313: ), 314: relative_path=match["file_path"], 315: source_code=source_code, 316: language=detect_language(match["file_path"]), 317: category=category, 318: repository_path=repository_info["path"], 319: line_count=match.get("line_end", match["line_start"]) - match["line_start"] + 1, 320: tags=[f"{BlockExtraction.FUNCTION_TAG_PREFIX}{function_name}"] 321: if function_name 322: else [], 323: ) 324: def extract_code_blocks( 325: pattern_matches: List[Dict], repository_info: Dict 326: ) -> List[CodeBlock]: 327: """Extract CodeBlock models from pattern matches.""" 328: _debug(f"extract_code_blocks: repository_info={repository_info}") 329: _debug(f"extract_code_blocks: got {len(pattern_matches)} pattern matches") 330: blocks = [] 331: for i, match in enumerate(pattern_matches): 332: if i == 0: 333: _debug( 334: f"first match: file_path={match.get('file_path')}, line_start={match.get('line_start')}" 335: ) 336: try: 337: block = _create_code_block(match, repository_info) 338: if i < BlockExtraction.DEBUG_LOG_BLOCK_LIMIT: 339: _debug( 340: f"block created: file={block.relative_path}, line={block.location.line_start}, tags={block.tags}" 341: ) 342: blocks.append(block) 343: except Exception as e: 344: import traceback 345: traceback.print_exc(file=sys.stderr) 346: continue 347: return blocks 348: def group_duplicates(blocks: List[CodeBlock]) -> List[DuplicateGroup]: 349: """ 350: Group similar code blocks using multi-layer similarity algorithm. 351: Priority 2: Structural Similarity 352: Uses the enhanced grouping algorithm that combines: 353: - Exact matching layer (hash-based) 354: - Structural similarity layer (AST-based) 355: - Semantic equivalence layer (category + tags) 356: """ 357: # Use the multi-layer grouping algorithm 358: groups = group_by_similarity( 359: blocks, similarity_threshold=SuggestionDefaults.GROUPING_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD 360: ) 361: return groups 362: def generate_suggestions(groups: List[DuplicateGroup]) -> List[ConsolidationSuggestion]: 363: """ 364: Generate consolidation suggestions with enhanced strategy logic 365: """ 366: suggestions = [] 367: for group in groups: 368: # Determine strategy based on Generate code example 373: code_example = _generate_code_example(group, strategy) 374: # Calculate ROI score (higher for simpler, lower-risk refactoring) 375: roi_score = _calculate_roi(group, complexity, risk) 376: # Determine if this is a breaking change 377: breaking_changes = _is_breaking_change(group, strategy) 378: suggestion = ConsolidationSuggestion( 379: suggestion_id=f"cs_{group.group_id}", 380: duplicate_group_id=group.group_id, 381: strategy=strategy, 382: strategy_rationale=rationale, 383: target_location=_suggest_target_location(group, strategy), 384: migration_steps=migration_steps, 385: code_example=code_example, 386: impact_score=min(group.impact_score, ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX), 387: complexity=complexity, 388: migration_risk=risk, 389: estimated_effort_hours=_estimate_effort(group, complexity), 390: breaking_changes=breaking_changes, 391: affected_files_count=len(group.affected_files), 392: affected_repositories_count=len(group.affected_repositories), 393: confidence=ConfidenceThresholds.HIGH_CONFIDENCE 394: if group.similarity_score >= ConfidenceThresholds.HIGH_SIMILARITY 395: else ConfidenceThresholds.LOW_CONFIDENCE, 396: roi_score=roi_score, 397: ) 398: suggestions.append(suggestion) 399: return suggestions 400: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 403: # Each category has rules with occurrence thresholds defining the strategy. 404: # Format: (max_occurrences, strategy, ), 464: StrategyRule( 465: None, 466: "mcp_server", 467: "API pattern used {occ} times - candidate for framework/MCP abstraction", 468: "complex", 469: "high", 470: ), 471: ], 472: "auth_check": [ list[StrategyRule] = [ 536: StrategyRule( 537: StrategyThresholds.DEFAULT_LOCAL_MAX, 538: "local_util", 539: "Utility pattern used {occ} times in {files} files - extract to local util", 540: "simple", 541: "minimal", 542: ), 543: StrategyRule( 544: StrategyThresholds.DEFAULT_SHARED_MAX, 545: "shared_package", 546: "Utility pattern used {occ} times across {files} files - create shared utility", 547: 557: ] 558: def _apply_strategy_rules( 559: rules: list[StrategyRule], 560: occurrences: int, 561: files: int, 562: ) -> tuple[str, str, str, str]: 563: for rule in rules: 564: if rule.max_occurrences is None or occurrences <= rule.max_occurrences: 565: rationale = rule.rationale_template.format(occ=occurrences, files=files) 566: return (rule.strategy, rationale, rule.complexity, rule.risk) 567: last_rule = rules[-1] 568: return ( 569: last_rule.strategy, 570: last_rule.rationale_template.format(occ=occurrences, files=files), 571: last_rule.complexity, 572: last_rule.risk, 573: ) 574: def _determine_strategy(group: DuplicateGroup) -> tuple[str, str, str, str]: 575: occurrences = group.occurrence_count 576: files = len(group.affected_files) 577: category = group.category 578: if files == 1: 579: return ( 580: "local_util", 581: f"All {occurrences} occurrences in same file - extract to local function", 582: "trivial", 583: "minimal", 584: ) 585: rules = 589: ) -> List[MigrationStep]: 590: if strategy == "local_util": 591: steps = [ 592: ("Create utility function in local utils module", True, "15min"), 593: ("Extract common logic from duplicate blocks", False, "30min"), 594: ("Replace each occurrence with function call", True, "20min"), 595: ("Add unit tests for extracted function", False, "30min"), 596: ("Run existing tests to verify behavior", True, "10min"), 597: ] 598: elif strategy == "shared_package": 599: steps = [ 600: ("Create shared package/module for utility", False, "1h"), 601: ("Extract and parameterize common logic", False, "1h"), 602: ("Add comprehensive tests to shared package", False, "45min"), 603: ("Update each file to import from shared package", True, "30min"), 604: ("Replace duplicates with shared function calls", True, "30min"), 605: ("Update package.json/requirements.txt dependencies", False, "15min"), 606: ("Run full test suite across affected projects", True, "20min"), 607: ] 608: elif strategy == "mcp_server": 609: steps = [ 610: ("Design MCP tool interface for functionality", False, "2h"), 611: ("Create MCP server with tool implementation", False, "4h"), 612: ("Add MCP tool schema and documentation", False, "1h"), 613: ("Test MCP tool independently", False, "1h"), 614: ("Update projects to use MCP client", False, "2h"), 615: ("Replace duplicates with MCP tool calls", True, "1h"), 616: ("Add integration tests", False, "2h"), 617: ("Document MCP tool usage", False, "1h"), 618: ] 619: else: 620: steps = [ 621: ("Define agent capabilities and workflow", False, "3h"), 622: ("Design agent prompt and tool access", False, "2h"), 623: ("Implement agent logic and orchestration", False, "8h"), 624: ("Create agent tests and safety checks", False, "3h"), 625: ("Integrate agent with existing systems", False, "4h"), 626: ("Replace complex duplicate logic with agent calls", False, "2h"), 627: ("Monitor agent performance and behavior", False, "ongoing"), 628: ("Document agent usage and limitations", False, "2h"), 629: ] 630: return [ 631: MigrationStep( 632: step_number=i + 1, 633: description=desc, 634: automated=automated, 635: estimated_time=time, 636: ) 637: for i, (desc, automated, time) in enumerate(steps) 638: ] 639: def _generate_code_example(group: DuplicateGroup, strategy: str) -> str: 640: category = group.category 641: if strategy == "local_util": 642: if "shared_package": 660: return """// Before: Duplicated across files 661: // file1.js: { check logic } 662: // file2.js: { check logic } 663: // After: Shared package 664: import { validateInput } from ... 670: } 671: // After: MCP tool 672: const result = await mcp.callTool('process-data', { input });""" 673: return "// Refactoring example not available" 674: def _calculate_roi(group: DuplicateGroup, complexity: str, risk: str) -> float: 675: roi = group.impact_score 676: complexity_multipliers = { 677: "trivial": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_TRIVIAL, 678: "simple": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_SIMPLE, 679: "moderate": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_MODERATE, 680: "complex": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_COMPLEX, 681: } 682: roi *= complexity_multipliers.get(complexity, 1.0) 683: risk_multipliers = { 684: "minimal": ROIMultipliers.RISK_MINIMAL, 685: "low": ROIMultipliers.RISK_LOW, 686: "medium": ROIMultipliers.RISK_MEDIUM, 687: "high": ROIMultipliers.RISK_HIGH, 688: } 689: roi *= risk_multipliers.get(risk, 1.0) 690: return min(roi, ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX) 691: def _is_breaking_change(group: DuplicateGroup, strategy: str) -> bool: 692: if strategy == DuplicateGroup, strategy: str) -> str: 698: if strategy == "local_util": 699: first_file = group.affected_files[0] if group.affected_files else "" 700: if "/" in first_file: 701: dir_path = "/".join(first_file.split("/")[:-1]) 702: return f"{dir_path}/utils.js" 703: return "utils.js" 704: elif strategy == "shared_package": 705: category = group.category 706: if category == "logger": 707: return "shared/logging/logger-utils.js" 708: elif category in ["api_handler", "auth_check"]: 709: return "shared/middleware/auth-middleware.js" 710: elif category == "database_operation": 711: return "shared/database/query-builder.js" 712: elif category == "validator": 713: return "shared/validation/validators.js" 714: else: 715: return f"shared/utils/{category}.js" 716: elif strategy == "mcp_server": 717: return f"mcp-servers/{group.pattern_id}-server/" 718: else: # autonomous_agent 719: return f"agents/{group.pattern_id}-agent/" 720: def _estimate_effort(group: DuplicateGroup, 726: hours = EFFORT_IMPLEMENTATION_DEFAULT_HOURS 727: # Add time per affected file (more files = more refactoring) 728: hours += len(group.affected_files) * blocks: List[CodeBlock], 734: groups: List[DuplicateGroup], 735: suggestions: List[ConsolidationSuggestion], 736: total_repo_lines: int = 0, 737: ) -> Dict[str, Any]: 738: """Calculate comprehensive duplication metrics. 739: Args: 740: blocks: All extracted code blocks 741: groups: All duplicate groups found 742: suggestions: Generated consolidation suggestions 743: total_repo_lines: Total lines in repository (for percentage calc) 744: Returns: 745: Dict with all metrics 746: """ 747: # Count by similarity method 748: exact_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "exact_match"] 749: structural_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "structural"] 750: semantic_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "semantic"] 751: total_duplicated_lines = sum(g.total_lines for g in groups) 752: potential_loc_reduction = sum( 753: g.total_lines - (g.total_lines // g.occurrence_count) for g in groups 754: ) 755: if total_repo_lines <= 0: 756: total_repo_lines = sum(b.line_count for b in blocks) 757: duplication_percentage = ( 758: (total_duplicated_lines / total_repo_lines * 100) 759: if total_repo_lines > 0 760: else 0.0 761: ) 762: quick_wins = [ 763: g 764: for g in groups 765: if g.occurrence_count <= ExtractionDefaults.QUICK_WIN_MAX_OCCURRENCES 766: and len(g.affected_files) == 1 767: ] 768: high_impact = [ 769: g 770: for g in groups 771: if g.total_lines >= ExtractionDefaults.HIGH_IMPACT_MIN_LINES 772: or g.occurrence_count >= ExtractionDefaults.HIGH_IMPACT_MIN_OCCURRENCES 773: ] 774: blocks_with_tags = sum(1 for b in blocks if b.tags) 775: total_tags = sum(len(b.tags) for b in blocks) 776: blocks_with_tags_percentage = ( 777: round(blocks_with_tags / len(blocks) * 100, 2) if blocks else 0.0 778: ) 779: avg_tags_per_block = round(total_tags / len(blocks), 2) if blocks else 0.0 780: return { 781: "total_code_blocks": len(blocks), 782: "total_duplicate_groups": len(groups), 783: "exact_duplicates": len(exact_groups), 784: "structural_duplicates": len(structural_groups), 785: "semantic_duplicates": len(semantic_groups), 786: "total_duplicated_lines": total_duplicated_lines, 787: "potential_loc_reduction": potential_loc_reduction, 788: "duplication_percentage": round(duplication_percentage, 2), 789: "total_suggestions": len(suggestions), 790: "quick_wins": len(quick_wins), 791: "high_impact_suggestions": len(high_impact), 792: "trivial_suggestions": len( 793: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "trivial"] 794: ), 795: "simple_suggestions": len([s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "simple"]), 796: "moderate_suggestions": len( 797: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "moderate"] 798: ), 799: "complex_suggestions": len( 800: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "complex"] 801: ), 802: "high_priority_suggestions": len( 803: [s for s in suggestions if s.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL] 804: ), 805: "blocks_with_tags": blocks_with_tags, 806: "blocks_with_tags_percentage": blocks_with_tags_percentage, 807: "avg_tags_per_block": avg_tags_per_block, 808: } 809: def main(): 810: try: 811: raw_input = 91: async createJobBranch(repositoryPath: string, jobContext: JobBranchContext): Promise<BranchResult> { 92: const span = Sentry.startInactiveSpan({ 93: op: 'git.create_branch', 94: name: 'Create Job jobId: commitContext.jobId }, 'Committing changes'); 147: const hasChanges = await this.hasChanges(repositoryPath); 148: if (!hasChanges) { 149: logger.info({ repositoryPath }, 'No changes to changed'); 155: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['add', '.']); 156: const commitMessage = this._generateCommitMessage(commitContext, changedFiles); 157: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, 'Changes committed'); 160: span?.setStatus('ok'); 161: return commitSha.trim(); 162: } catch (error) { 163: span?.setStatus('internal_error'); 164: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to commit changes', Skipping branch push'); 189: span?.setStatus('ok'); 190: return false; 191: } 192: logger.info({ repositoryPath, branchName }, 'Pushing branch to remote'); 193: await runGitCommand(repositoryPath, ['push', '-u', 'origin', branchName]); 194: logger.info({ branchName }, 'Branch pushed successfully'); 195: span?.setStatus('ok'); 196: return true; 197: } catch (error) { 198: span?.setStatus('internal_error'); 199: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to push branch', { repositoryPath, branchName }); 200: Sentry.captureException(error, { 201: tags: { 202: component: 'branch-manager', 203: operation: 'push' 204: }, 205: extra: { 206: repositoryPath, 207: branchName 208: } 209: }); 210: return false; 211: } finally { 212: if (span) span.end(); 213: } 214: } 215: 219: }); 220: try { 221: if (this.dryRun) { 222: logger.info({ branch: prContext.branchName }, 'Dry run: Skipping PR creation'); 223: span?.setStatus('ok'); 224: return `dry-run-${prContext.branchName}`; 225: } 226: logger.info({ 227: repositoryPath, 228: branchName: prContext.branchName, 229: title: prContext.title 230: }, 'Creating pull request'); 231: const args = [ 232: 'pr', 233: 'create', 234: '--title', prContext.title, 235: '--body', prContext.body, 236: '--base', this.baseBranch, 237: '--head', prContext.branchName 238: ]; 239: if (prContext.labels && requires_human_review: bool = Field( 113: True, description="Requires human review before implementation" 114: ) 115: benefits: List[str] = Field( 116: default_factory=list, description="List of benefits from this consolidation" 117: ) 118: drawbacks: List[str] = Field( 119: default_factory=list, description="Potential drawbacks or concerns" 120: ) 121: notes: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Additional notes") 122: created_at: datetime = Field( 123: default_factory=utc_now, description="When this suggestion was created" 124: ) 125: model_config = { 126: "json_schema_extra": { 127: "example": { 128: "suggestion_id": "cs_001", 129: "duplicate_group_id": "dg_001", 130: "strategy": "local_util", 131: "strategy_rationale": "Used within single project, simple utility function", 132: "impact_score": 75.0, 133: "complexity": "trivial", 134: "migration_risk": "low", 135: "breaking_changes": False, 136: "target_location": "src/utils/json.js", 137: "target_name": "writeJsonFile", 138: "loc_reduction": 15, 139: "affected_files_count": 5, 140: "affected_repositories_count": 1, 141: } 142: } 143: } 144: @field_validator("impact_score") 145: @classmethod 146: def round_impact_score(cls, v): 147: return round(v, 2) 148: @computed_field 149: @property 150: def priority(self) -> str: 151: if self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL and self.complexity in [ 152: "trivial", 153: "simple", 154: ]: 155: return "critical" 156: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.HIGH and self.complexity in [ 157: "trivial", 158: "simple", 159: "moderate", 160: ]: 161: return "high" 162: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.MEDIUM: 163: return "medium" 164: else: 165: return "low" 166: @computed_field 167: @property 168: def roi_score(self) -> float: 169: effort_tier = EffortTier(self.complexity.value) 170: effort = self.estimated_effort_hours or EFFORT_ROI_HOURS_BY_TIER.get( 171: effort_tier, SuggestionDefaults.DEFAULT_EFFORT_FALLBACK 172: ) 173: if effort == 0: 174: return ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX 175: roi = (self.impact_score / effort) * SuggestionDefaults.ROI_NORMALIZER 176: return min(round(roi, 2), ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX) 177: @computed_field 178: @property 179: def is_quick_win(self) -> bool: 180: return ( 181: self.impact_score >= SuggestionDefaults.QUICK_WIN_MIN_IMPACT 182: and self.complexity 183: in [ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, ImplementationComplexity.SIMPLE] 184: and self.migration_risk in [MigrationRisk.MINIMAL, MigrationRisk.LOW] 185: ) 186: def add_migration_step( 187: self, 188: description: str, 189: code_example: Optional[str] = None, 190: automated: bool = False, 191: estimated_time: Optional[str] = None, 192: ) -> None: 193: step_number = len(self.migration_steps) + 1 194: step = MigrationStep( 195: step_number=step_number, 196: description=description, 197: code_example=code_example, 198: automated=automated, 199: estimated_time=estimated_time, 200: ) 201: self.migration_steps.append(step) 202: def add_benefit(self, benefit: str) -> None: 203: if benefit not in self.benefits: 204: self.benefits.append(benefit) 205: def add_drawback(self, drawback: str) -> None: 206: if drawback not in self.drawbacks: 207: self.drawbacks.append(drawback) 208: def to_markdown_summary(self) -> str: 209: return f"""## {self.target_name or "Consolidation Suggestion"} 210: **Strategy:** {self.strategy.value.replace("_", " ").title()} 211: **Priority:** {self.priority.upper()} 212: **Impact Score:** {self.impact_score}/100 213: **ROI Score:** {self.roi_score}/100 214: ### Rationale 215: {self.strategy_rationale} 216: ### Metrics 217: - **Complexity:** {self.complexity.value} 218: - **Risk:** {self.migration_risk.value} 219: - **LOC Reduction:** {self.loc_reduction or "Unknown"} 220: - **Files Affected:** {self.affected_files_count} 221: - **Breaking Changes:** {"Yes" if self.breaking_changes else "No"} 222: ### Benefits 223: {chr(10).join(f"- {b}" for b in self.benefits) if self.benefits else "None specified"} 224: ### Migration Steps 225: {chr(10).join(f"{i + 1}. {step.description}" for i, step in enumerate(self.migration_steps)) if self.migration_steps else "Not specified"} 226: """ 227: def __hash__(self) -> int: 228: return hash(self.suggestion_id) 229: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: 230: if not isinstance(other, ConsolidationSuggestion): 231: return NotImplemented 232: return self.suggestion_id == other.suggestion_id 1: """Type stubs for consolidation_suggestion module."""
