deduplication_potential(self) -> int: 94: if self.occurrence_count <= 1: 95: return 0 96: avg_lines_per_instance = self.total_lines / self.occurrence_count 97: return int((self.occurrence_count - 1) * avg_lines_per_instance) 98: @computed_field 99: @property 100: def impact_score(self) -> float: 101: occurrence_factor = min( 102: self.occurrence_count / ImpactWeights.OCCURRENCE_CAP, 1.0 103: ) 104: similarity_factor = self.similarity_score 105: loc_factor = min(self.total_lines / ImpactWeights.LOC_CAP, 1.0) 106: score = ( 107: occurrence_factor * ImpactWeights.OCCURRENCE 108: + similarity_factor * ImpactWeights.SIMILARITY 109: + loc_factor * ImpactWeights.SIZE 110: ) 111: return round(score, 2) 112: @computed_field 113: @property 114: def is_cross_repository(self) -> bool: 115: return len(self.affected_repositories) > 1 116: @computed_field 117: @property 118: def priority_level(self) -> str: 119: if self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL: 120: return "critical" 121: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.HIGH: 122: return "high" 123: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.MEDIUM: 124: return "medium" 125: else: 126: return "low" 127: def add_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: 128: if block_id not in self.member_block_ids: 129: self.member_block_ids.append(block_id) 130: self.occurrence_count = len(self.member_block_ids) 131: self.updated_at = utc_now() 132: def remove_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: 133: if block_id in self.member_block_ids: 134: self.member_block_ids.remove(block_id) 135: self.occurrence_count = len(self.member_block_ids) 136: self.updated_at = utc_now() 137: if self.canonical_block_id == block_id: 138: self.canonical_block_id = None 139: def set_canonical(self, block_id: str) -> None: 140: if block_id not in self.member_block_ids: 141: raise ValueError(f"Block {block_id} is not a member of this group") 142: self.canonical_block_id = block_id 143: self.updated_at = utc_now() 144: def __hash__(self) -> int: 145: return hash(self.group_id) 146: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: 147: if not isinstance(other, DuplicateGroup): 148: return NotImplemented 149: return self.group_id == other.group_id 1: """Type stubs for duplicate_group module.""" 2: 3: from datetime import datetime 4: from enum import Enum 5: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional 6: 7: from pydantic import BaseModel 8: 9: class SimilarityMethod(str, Enum): 10: EXACT_MATCH = ... 11: STRUCTURAL = ... 12: SEMANTIC = ... 13: HYBRID = ... 14: 15: class DuplicateGroup(BaseModel): 16: group_id: str 17: pattern_id: str 18: member_block_ids: List[str] 19: similarity_score: float 20: similarity_method: SimilarityMethod 21: canonical_block_id: Optional[str] 22: category: str 23: language: str 24: occurrence_count: int 25: total_lines: int 26: affected_files: List[str] 27: affected_repositories: List[str] 28: consolidation_complexity: Optional[str] 29: breaking_changes_risk: Optional[str] 30: created_at: datetime 31: updated_at: datetime 32: notes: Optional[str] 33: metadata: Dict[str, Any] 34: 35: @property 36: def deduplication_potential(self) -> int: ... 37: @property 38: def impact_score(self) -> float: ... 39: @property 40: def is_cross_repository(self) -> bool: ... 41: @property 42: def priority_level(self) -> str: ... 43: def add_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 44: def remove_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 45: def set_canonical(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 46: def __hash__(self) -> int: ... 47: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: ... 1: # Pydantic Models for Duplicate Detection 2: 3: Data models for structuring duplicate detection and consolidation results using Pydantic v2. 4: 5: ## Overview 6: 7: These models define the data structures used throughout the duplicate detection pipeline, from individual code blocks to complete scan reports with consolidation recommendations. 8: 9: ## Model Hierarchy 10: 11: ``` 12: ScanReport (top-level) 13: ├── RepositoryInfo[] # Scanned repositories 14: ├── ScanConfiguration # Scan settings 15: ├── ScanMetrics # Statistical summary 16: ├── code_block_ids[] # → CodeBlock references 17: ├── duplicate_group_ids[] # → DuplicateGroup references 18: └── suggestion_ids[] # → ConsolidationSuggestion references 19: 20: CodeBlock # Individual code pattern 21: ├── SourceLocation # File + line location 22: ├── ASTNode # AST tree structure 23: ├── LanguageType # Programming language enum 24: └── SemanticCategory # Pattern category enum 25: 26: DuplicateGroup # Similar code blocks 27: ├── member_block_ids[] # CodeBlock references 28: ├── SimilarityMethod # How similarity was determined 29: └── computed: impact_score, priority_level 30: 31: ConsolidationSuggestion # Refactoring recommendation 32: ├── ConsolidationStrategy # Tier: local_util → autonomous_agent 33: ├── ImplementationComplexity 34: ├── MigrationRisk 35: ├── MigrationStep[] # Step-by-step guide 36: └── computed: priority, roi_score, is_quick_win 37: ``` 38: 39: ## Models 40: 41: ### CodeBlock 42: 43: Represents a code pattern detected by ast-grep. 44: 45: | Field | Type | Description | 46: |-------|------|-------------| 47: | `block_id` | str | Unique identifier | 48: | `pattern_id` | str | ast-grep rule ID | 49: | `location` | SourceLocation | File path + line numbers | 50: | `source_code` | str | Raw source code | 51: | `language` | LanguageType | javascript, typescript, python, etc. | 52: | `category` | SemanticCategory | utility, api_handler, database_operation, etc. | 53: | `content_hash` | computed | SHA-256 hash for exact matching | 54: | `structural_hash` | computed | AST-based hash for structural matching | 55: 56: ### DuplicateGroup 57: 58: Groups similar CodeBlocks as consolidation candidates. 59: 60: | Field | Type | Description | 61: |-------|------|-------------| 62: | `group_id` | str | Unique identifier | 63: | `member_block_ids` | List[str] | CodeBlock IDs (min 2) | 64: | `similarity_score` | float | 0.0-1.0 similarity | 65: | `similarity_method` | SimilarityMethod | exact_match, structural, semantic, hybrid | 66: | `occurrence_count` | int | Number of instances | 67: | `deduplication_potential` | computed | Lines removable | 68: | `impact_score` | computed | 0-100 consolidation priority | 69: | `priority_level` | computed | critical, high, medium, low | 70: 71: ### ConsolidationSuggestion 72: 73: Actionable recommendation for consolidating duplicates. 74: 75: | Field | Type | Description | 76: |-------|------|-------------| 77: | `suggestion_id` | str | Unique identifier | 78: | `duplicate_group_id` | str | Target DuplicateGroup | 79: | `strategy` | ConsolidationStrategy | local_util, shared_package, mcp_server, autonomous_agent | 80: | `complexity` | ImplementationComplexity | trivial, simple, moderate, complex, very_complex | 81: | `migration_risk` | MigrationRisk | minimal, low, medium, high, critical | 82: | `migration_steps` | List[MigrationStep] | Step-by-step implementation guide | 83: | `priority` | computed | Based on impact + complexity | 84: | `roi_score` | computed | Return on investment (0-100) | 85: | `is_quick_win` | computed | High impact + low effort + low risk | 86: 87: ### ScanReport 88: 89: Top-level report containing all scan results. 90: 91: | Field | Type | Description | 92: |-------|------|-------------| 93: | `report_id` | str | Unique identifier | 94: | `repositories` | List[RepositoryInfo] | Scanned repos | 95: | `configuration` | ScanConfiguration | Rules and thresholds used | 96: | `metrics` | ScanMetrics | Statistical summary | 97: | `duplication_severity` | computed | minimal, low, moderate, high, critical | 98: | `consolidation_opportunity_score` | computed | 0-100 benefit score | 99: 100: ## Enums 101: 102: ### LanguageType 103: ```python 104: JAVASCRIPT, TYPESCRIPT, PYTHON, JAVA, GO, RUST, C, CPP, CSHARP, PHP, RUBY 105: ``` 106: 107: ### SemanticCategory 108: ```python 109: UTILITY, HELPER, VALIDATOR, API_HANDLER, AUTH_CHECK, DATABASE_OPERATION, 110: ERROR_HANDLER, LOGGER, CONFIG_ACCESS, FILE_OPERATION, ASYNC_PATTERN, UNKNOWN 111: ``` 112: 113: ### SimilarityMethod 114: ```python 115: EXACT_MATCH # Identical code 116: STRUCTURAL # Same AST structure 117: SEMANTIC # Similar logic/behavior 118: HYBRID # Combination of methods 119: ``` 120: 121: ### ConsolidationStrategy 122: ```python 123: LOCAL_UTIL # Utility within single project 124: SHARED_PACKAGE # Shared library across 2-3 projects 125: MCP_SERVER # MCP server for cross-language/tool 126: AUTONOMOUS_AGENT # Complex orchestration requiring AI 127: NO_ACTION # Not worth consolidating 128: ``` 129: 130: ## Usage 131: 132: ### Import Models 133: 134: ```python 135: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 136: CodeBlock, 137: DuplicateGroup, 138: ConsolidationSuggestion, 139: ScanReport, 140: LanguageType, 141: SemanticCategory, 142: SimilarityMethod, 143: ) 144: ``` 145: 146: ### Create CodeBlock 147: 148: ```python 149: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 150: CodeBlock, SourceLocation, LanguageType, SemanticCategory 151: ) 152: 153: block = CodeBlock( 154: block_id="cb_001", 155: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 156: location=SourceLocation( 157: file_path="/path/to/repo/src/utils.js", 158: line_start=42, 159: line_end=44 160: ), 161: relative_path="src/utils.js", 162: source_code="JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)", 163: language=LanguageType.JAVASCRIPT, 164: category=SemanticCategory.UTILITY, 165: repository_path="/path/to/repo", 166: line_count=3 167: ) 168: 169: # Access computed hashes 170: print(block.content_hash) # e.g., "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." 171: print(block.structural_hash) # Falls back to content_hash if no AST 172: ``` 173: 174: ### Create DuplicateGroup 175: 176: ```python 177: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import DuplicateGroup, SimilarityMethod 178: 179: group = DuplicateGroup( 180: group_id="dg_001", 181: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 182: member_block_ids=["cb_001", "cb_002", "cb_003"], 183: similarity_score=0.95, 184: similarity_method=SimilarityMethod.STRUCTURAL, 185: category="utility", 186: language="javascript", 187: occurrence_count=3, 188: total_lines=9, 189: affected_files=["src/utils.js", "src/helpers.js"] 190: ) 191: 192: # Access computed fields 193: print(group.deduplication_potential) # Lines removable: 6 194: print(group.impact_score) # 0-100 priority score 195: print(group.priority_level) # "high" 196: print(group.is_cross_repository) # False 197: ``` 198: 199: ### Create ConsolidationSuggestion 200: 201: ```python 202: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 203: ConsolidationSuggestion, 204: ConsolidationStrategy, 205: ImplementationComplexity, 206: MigrationRisk 207: ) 208: 209: suggestion = ConsolidationSuggestion( 210: suggestion_id="cs_001", 211: duplicate_group_id="dg_001", 212: strategy=ConsolidationStrategy.LOCAL_UTIL, 213: strategy_rationale="Used within single project, simple utility function", 214: impact_score=75.0, 215: complexity=ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, 216: migration_risk=MigrationRisk.LOW, 217: breaking_changes=False, 218: target_location="src/utils/json.js", 219: target_name="writeJsonFile", 220: loc_reduction=15, 221: affected_files_count=5, 222: affected_repositories_count=1, 223: confidence=0.9 224: ) 225: 226: # Access computed fields 227: print(suggestion.priority) # "critical" 228: print(suggestion.roi_score) # Return on investment 229: print(suggestion.is_quick_win) # True 230: 231: # Add migration steps 232: suggestion.add_migration_step( 233: description="Create writeJsonFile function in src/utils/json.js", 234: code_example="export function writeJsonFile(path, data) { ... }", 235: automated=True, 236: estimated_time="15min" 237: ) 238: 239: # Generate markdown summary 240: print(suggestion.to_markdown_summary()) 241: ``` 242: 243: ### Export for Comparison 244: 245: ```python 246: # Export minimal data for clustering algorithms 247: comparison_data = block.to_dict_for_comparison() 248: # Returns: { block_id, pattern_id, category, language, content_hash, ... } 249: 250: # Export summary for dashboards 251: summary = report.to_summary_dict() 252: # Returns: { report_id, duplication_percentage, quick_wins, ... } 253: ``` 254: 255: ## Validation 256: 257: Models include Pydantic validators: 258: 259: ```python 260: # Line range validation 261: SourceLocation(file_path="x.js", line_start=10, line_end=5) 262: # ValidationError: line_end must be >= line_start 263: 264: # Minimum members validation 265: DuplicateGroup(member_block_ids=["cb_001"]) # Only 1 member 266: # ValidationError: A duplicate group must have at least 2 members 267: 268: # Canonical block validation 269: group.set_canonical("cb_999") # Not in member_block_ids 270: # ValueError: Block cb_999 is not a member of this group 271: ``` 272: 273: ## JSON Schema 274: 275: All models support JSON schema export for API documentation: 276: 277: ```python 278: import json 279: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import CodeBlock 280: 281: schema = CodeBlock.model_json_schema() 282: print(json.dumps(schema, indent=2)) 283: ``` 284: 285: ## Related Documentation 286: 287: - [Similarity Algorithm](../../../docs/architecture/similarity-algorithm.md) - How duplicates are detected 288: - [Pipeline Data Flow](../../../docs/architecture/pipeline-data-flow.md) - How models flow through pipeline 289: - [AST-Grep Rules](../../../.ast-grep/README.md) - Pattern detection rules 290: 291: --- 292: 293: **Version:** 1.0.0 294: **Pydantic:** v2 295: **Python:** 3.10+ 1: import sys 2: from datetime import UTC, datetime 3: from pathlib import Path 4: from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any 5: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, computed_field 6: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent)) 7: from constants import DuplicationThresholds, OpportunityWeights, ScanDefaults 8: def utc_now() -> datetime: 9: return datetime.now(UTC) 10: class RepositoryInfo(BaseModel): 11: repository_path: str = Field(..., description="Absolute path to repository") 12: repository_name: str = Field(..., description="Repository name/identifier") 13: git_remote: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Git remote URL") 14: git_branch: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Current branch") 15: git_commit: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Current commit hash") 16: total_files: int = Field(..., ge=0, description="Total files scanned") 17: total_lines: int = Field(..., ge=0, description="Total lines of code scanned") 18: languages: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Languages detected") 19: class ScanConfiguration(BaseModel): 20: rules_used: List[str] = Field( 21: default_factory=list, description="ast-grep rules applied" 22: ) 23: excluded_paths: List[str] = Field( 24: default_factory=list, description="Paths excluded from scan" 25: ) 26: min_similarity_threshold: float = Field( 27: ScanDefaults.MIN_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, 28: ge=0.0, 29: le=1.0, 30: description="Minimum similarity for grouping", 31: ) 32: min_duplicate_size: int = Field( 33: ScanDefaults.MIN_DUPLICATE_SIZE, 34: ge=1, 35: description="Minimum lines for duplicate detection", 36: ) 37: class ScanMetrics(BaseModel): 38: total_code_blocks: int = Field(..., ge=0, description="Total code blocks detected") 39: code_blocks_by_category: Dict[str, int] = Field( 40: default_factory=dict, description="Count of blocks by semantic category" 41: ) 42: code_blocks_by_language: Dict[str, int] = Field( 43: default_factory=dict, description="Count of blocks by programming language" 44: ) 45: total_duplicate_groups: int = Field( 46: ..., ge=0, description="Total duplicate groups found" 47: ) 48: exact_duplicates: int = Field(..., ge=0, description="Groups with 100% similarity") 49: structural_duplicates: int = Field( 50: ..., ge=0, description="Groups with structural similarity" 51: ) 52: semantic_duplicates: int = Field( 53: ..., ge=0, description="Groups with semantic similarity" 54: ) 55: total_duplicated_lines: int = Field( 56: ..., ge=0, description="Total lines in duplicate groups" 57: ) 58: potential_loc_reduction: int = Field( 59: ..., ge=0, description="Potential lines that could be removed" 60: ) 61: duplication_percentage: float = Field( 62: ..., 63: ge=0.0, 64: le=ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX, 65: description="Percentage of code that's duplicated", 66: ) 67: total_suggestions: int = Field( 68: ..., ge=0, description="Total consolidation suggestions" 69: ) 70: quick_wins: int = Field(..., ge=0, description="Number of quick win suggestions") 71: high_priority_suggestions: int = Field( 72: ..., ge=0, description="High priority suggestions" 73: ) 74: cross_repository_duplicates: int = Field( 75: 0, ge=0, description="Duplicates spanning multiple repos" 76: ) 77: class ScanReport(BaseModel): 78: report_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique identifier for this report") 79: scan_name: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Descriptive name for this scan") 80: scanned_at: datetime = Field( 81: default_factory=utc_now, description="Scan timestamp" 82: ) 83: scan_duration_seconds: Optional[float] = Field( 84: None, ge=0, description="How long the scan took" 85: ) 86: scanner_version: str = Field( 87: "1.0.0", description="Version of duplicate detection pipeline" 88: ) 89: configuration: ScanConfiguration = Field(..., description="Scan configuration") 90: repositories: List[RepositoryInfo] = Field(..., description="Repositories scanned") 91: code_block_ids: List[str] = Field( 92: default_factory=list, description="IDs of detected code blocks" 93: ) 94: duplicate_group_ids: List[str] = Field( 95: default_factory=list, description="IDs of duplicate groups" 96: ) 97: suggestion_ids: List[str] = Field( 98: default_factory=list, description="IDs of consolidation suggestions" 99: ) 100: metrics: ScanMetrics = Field(..., description="Statistical metrics") 101: executive_summary: Optional[str] = Field( 102: None, description="High-level summary of findings" 103: ) 104: recommendations: List[str] = Field( 105: default_factory=list, description="Top-level recommendations" 106: ) 107: warnings: List[str] = Field( 108: default_factory=list, description="Warnings or issues encountered" 109: ) 110: output_directory: str = Field( 111: ..., description="Directory where detailed results are saved" 112: ) 113: report_files: Dict[str, str] = Field( 114: default_factory=dict, 115: description="Paths to generated report files (HTML, JSON, etc.)", 116: ) 117: tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Tags for categorization") 118: metadata: Dict[str, Any] = Field( 119: default_factory=dict, description="Additional metadata" 120: ) 121: model_config = { 122: "json_schema_extra": { 123: "example": { 124: "report_id": "scan_20250111_001", 125: "scan_name": "Sidequest Repository Scan", 126: "repositories": [ 127: { 128: "repository_name": "sidequest", 129: "repository_path": "/Users/user/code/jobs/sidequest", 130: "total_files": 15, 131: "total_lines": 2500, 132: "languages": ["javascript", "typescript"], 133: } 134: ], 135: "metrics": { 136: "total_code_blocks": 150, 137: "total_duplicate_groups": 12, 138: "total_duplicated_lines": 300, 139: "potential_loc_reduction": 250, 140: "duplication_percentage": 12.0, 141: "total_suggestions": 12, 142: "quick_wins": 5, 143: }, 144: } 145: } 146: } 147: @computed_field 148: @property 149: def is_multi_repository(self) -> bool: 150: return len(self.repositories) > 1 151: @computed_field 152: @property 153: def total_scanned_files(self) -> int: 154: return sum(repo.total_files for repo in self.repositories) 155: @computed_field 156: @property 157: def total_scanned_lines(self) -> int: 158: return sum(repo.total_lines for repo in self.repositories) 159: @computed_field 160: @property 161: def duplication_severity(self) -> str: 162: dup_pct = self.metrics.duplication_percentage 163: if dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.MINIMAL_PCT: 164: return "minimal" 165: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.LOW_PCT: 166: return "low" 167: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.MODERATE_PCT: 168: return "moderate" 169: elif dup_pct < DuplicationThresholds.HIGH_PCT: 170: return "high" 171: else: 172: return "critical" 173: @computed_field 174: @property 175: def consolidation_opportunity_score(self) -> float: 176: dup_factor = min( 177: self.metrics.duplication_percentage 178: / DuplicationThresholds.HIGH_PCT 179: * ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX, 180: ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX, 181: ) 182: quick_win_factor = min( 183: self.metrics.quick_wins 184: / DuplicationThresholds.QUICK_WIN_CAP 185: * ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX, 186: ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX, 187: ) 188: if self.total_scanned_lines > 0: 189: loc_reduction_factor = ( 190: self.metrics.potential_loc_reduction 191: / self.total_scanned_lines 192: * ScanDefaults.PERCENTAGE_MAX 193: ) 194: else: 195: loc_reduction_factor = 0 196: score = ( 197: dup_factor * OpportunityWeights.DUPLICATION 198: + quick_win_factor * OpportunityWeights.QUICK_WIN 199: + loc_reduction_factor * OpportunityWeights.LOC_REDUCTION 200: ) 201: return round(min(score, 100), 2) 202: def add_repository(self, repo_info: RepositoryInfo) -> None: 203: self.repositories.append(repo_info) 204: def add_code_block_id(self, block_id: str) -> None: 205: if block_id not in self.code_block_ids: 206: self.code_block_ids.append(block_id) 207: def add_duplicate_group_id(self, group_id: str) -> None: 208: if group_id not in self.duplicate_group_ids: 209: self.duplicate_group_ids.append(group_id) 210: def add_suggestion_id(self, suggestion_id: str) -> None: 211: if suggestion_id not in self.suggestion_ids: 212: self.suggestion_ids.append(suggestion_id) 213: def generate_executive_summary(self) -> str: 214: repos_text = ( 215: f"{len(self.repositories)} repository" 216: if len(self.repositories) == 1 217: else f"{len(self.repositories)} repositories" 218: ) 219: summary = f""" 220: # Duplicate Detection Scan Report 221: Scanned {repos_text} containing {self.total_scanned_files:,} files and { 222: self.total_scanned_lines:,} lines of code. 223: ## Key Findings 224: - **Duplicate Groups Found:** {self.metrics.total_duplicate_groups} 225: - **Duplicated Code:** {self.metrics.total_duplicated_lines:,} lines ({ 226: self.metrics.duplication_percentage:.1f}% of total) 227: - **Duplication Severity:** {self.duplication_severity.upper()} 228: - **Potential Reduction:** { 229: self.metrics.potential_loc_reduction:,} lines could be eliminated 230: ## Consolidation Opportunities 231: - **Total Suggestions:** {self.metrics.total_suggestions} 232: - **Quick Wins:** {self.metrics.quick_wins} high-impact, low-effort consolidations 233: - **High Priority:** {self.metrics.high_priority_suggestions} high-priority suggestions 234: - **Opportunity Score:** {self.consolidation_opportunity_score}/100 235: ## Recommendation 236: { 237: "🚀 Immediate action recommended - many quick wins available!" 238: if self.metrics.quick_wins >= ScanDefaults.QUICK_WINS_RECOMMEND_THRESHOLD 239: else "⚠️ Moderate duplication detected - consider prioritizing high-impact consolidations" 240: if self.metrics.duplication_percentage >= ScanDefaults.HIGH_DUPLICATION_PCT 241: else "✅ Low duplication - focus on preventing new duplicates" 242: } 243: """ 244: return summary.strip() 245: def to_summary_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: 246: return { 247: "report_id": self.report_id, 248: "scanned_at": self.scanned_at.isoformat(), 249: "repositories_count": len(self.repositories), 250: "total_files": self.total_scanned_files, 251: "total_lines": self.total_scanned_lines, 252: "duplication_percentage": self.metrics.duplication_percentage, 253: "duplication_severity": self.duplication_severity, 254: "duplicate_groups": self.metrics.total_duplicate_groups, 255: "potential_loc_reduction": self.metrics.potential_loc_reduction, 256: "suggestions_total": self.metrics.total_suggestions, 257: "quick_wins": self.metrics.quick_wins, 258: "opportunity_score": self.consolidation_opportunity_score, 259: } 260: def __hash__(self) -> int: 261: return hash(self.report_id) 262: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: 263: if not isinstance(other, ScanReport): 264: return NotImplemented 265: return self.report_id == other.report_id 1: """Type stubs for scan_report module.""" 2: 3: from datetime import 'Inter-Project' : 'Intra-Project'} 52: </span> 53: <span class="meta-item"> 54: <strong>Date:</strong> ${scanDate.toLocaleString()} 55: </span> 56: <span class="meta-item"> 57: <strong>Duration:</strong> ${formatDuration(duration)} 58: </span> 59: </div> 60: </header>`; 61: } 62: private static _generateMetrics(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 63: const metrics = scanResult.metrics ?? {}; 64: if (isInterProject) { 65: return ` 66: <section class="metrics"> 67: <h2>📊 Scan Metrics</h2> 68: <div class="metrics-grid"> 69: <div class="metric-card"> 70: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_repositories_scanned ?? 0}</div> 71: <div class="metric-label">Repositories Scanned</div> 72: </div> 73: <div class="metric-card"> 74: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0}</div> 75: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks</div> 76: </div> 77: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 78: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_cross_repository_groups ?? 0}</div> 79: <div class="metric-label">Cross-Repo Duplicates</div> 80: </div> 81: <div class="metric-card"> 82: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.cross_repository_duplicated_lines ?? 0}</div> 83: <div class="metric-label">Duplicated Lines</div> 84: </div> 85: <div class="metric-card success"> 86: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.shared_package_candidates ?? 0}</div> 102: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks Detected</div> 103: </div> 104: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 105: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_duplicate_groups ?? 0}</div> 106: <div class="metric-label">Duplicate Groups</div> 107: </div> 108: <div class="metric-card"> 109: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.exact_duplicates ?? (scanResult.cross_repository_suggestions ?? []) 130: : (scanResult.suggestions ?? []); 131: const strategyCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 132: suggestions.forEach(s => { 133: strategyCounts[s.strategy] = (strategyCounts[s.strategy] ?? 0) + 1; 134: }); 135: const complexityCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 136: suggestions.forEach(s => { 137: complexityCounts[s.complexity] = (complexityCounts[s.complexity] ?? 0) + 1; 138: }); 139: return ` 140: <section class="charts"> 141: <h2>📈 Distribution</h2> 142: <div class="charts-grid"> 143: <div class="chart-card"> 144: <h3>By Strategy</h3> 145: <div class="chart-bars"> 146: ${Object.entries(strategyCounts).map(([strategy, count]) => ` 147: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 148: <span class="chart-label">${strategy.replace('_', ' ')}</span> 149: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 150: <div class="chart-bar strategy-${strategy}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 151: </div> 152: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 153: </div> 154: `).join('')} 155: </div> 156: </div> 157: <div class="chart-card"> 158: <h3>By Complexity</h3> 159: <div class="chart-bars"> 160: ${Object.entries(complexityCounts).map(([complexity, count]) => ` 161: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 162: <span class="chart-label">${complexity}</span> 163: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 164: <div class="chart-bar complexity-${complexity}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 165: </div> 166: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 167: </div> 168: `).join('')} 169: </div> 170: </div> 171: </div> 172: </section>`; 173: } 174: private static _generateCrossRepoSection(scanResult: ScanResult): string { 175: const repos = scanResult.scanned_repositories ?? 'error' : ''}"> 182: <div class="repo-name">${escapeHtml(repo.name)}</div> 183: ${repo.error ? ` 184: <div class="repo-error">❌ ${escapeHtml(repo.error)}</div> 185: ` : ` 186: <div class="repo-stats"> 187: <span>${repo.code_blocks ?? 0} blocks</span> 188: <span>${repo.duplicate_groups ?? 0} groups</span> 189: </div> 190: `} 191: </div> 192: `).join('')} 193: </div> 194: </section>`; 195: } 196: private static _generateDuplicateGroups(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 197: const groups = isInterProject 198: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_duplicates ?? []) 199: : (scanResult.duplicate_groups ?? []); 200: const topGroups = groups 201: .sort((a, b) => b.impact_score - a.impact_score) 202: .slice(0, 10); 203: if (topGroups.length === 0) { 204: return ` 205: <section class="duplicates"> 206: <h2>🎯 Duplicate Groups</h2> 207: <p class="empty-state">No duplicate groups detected.</p> 208: </section>`; 209: } 210: return ` 211: <section class="duplicates"> 212: <h2>🎯 Top Duplicate Groups</h2> 213: <div class="duplicates-list"> 214: ${topGroups.map((group, index) => ` 215: <div class="duplicate-card"> 216: <div class="duplicate-header"> 217: <div class="duplicate-rank">#${index + 1}</div> 218: <div class="duplicate-title"> 219: <strong>${escapeHtml(group.group_id)}</strong> 220: <span class="duplicate-pattern">${escapeHtml(group.pattern_id)}</span> 221: </div> 222: <div class="duplicate-impact ${this._getImpactClass(group.impact_score)}"> 223: ${group.impact_score.toFixed(0)}% impact 224: </div> 225: </div> 226: <div class="duplicate-stats"> 227: <span class="stat-badge"> 228: 📦 ${group.occurrence_count} occurrences 229: </span> 230: ${isInterProject ? ` 231: <span class="stat-badge"> 232: 🔗 ${group.repository_count ?? 0} repositories 233: </span> 234: ` : ''} 235: <span class="stat-badge"> 236: 📄 ${group.affected_files?.length ?? 0} files 237: </span> 238: <span class="stat-badge"> 239: 📏 ${group.total_lines} lines 240: </span> 241: </div> 242: <div class="duplicate-files"> 243: <strong>Affected files:</strong> 244: <ul> 245: ${(group.affected_files ?? 'breaking' : ''}"> 280: <div class="suggestion-header"> 281: <div class="suggestion-rank">#${index + 1}</div> 282: <div class="suggestion-title"> 283: <strong>${escapeHtml(suggestion.suggestion_id)}</strong> 284: <span class="suggestion-strategy strategy-${suggestion.strategy}"> 285: ${escapeHtml(suggestion.strategy.replace('_', ' '))} 286: </span> 287: </div> 288: <div class="suggestion-roi ${this._getROIClass(suggestion.roi_score)}"> 289: ROI: ${suggestion.roi_score.toFixed(0)}% 290: </div> 291: </div> 292: <div class="suggestion-body"> 293: <p class="suggestion-rationale">${escapeHtml(suggestion.strategy_rationale ?? '')}</p> 294: ${suggestion.target_location ? ` 295: <p class="suggestion-target"> 296: <strong>Target:</strong> <code>${escapeHtml(suggestion.target_location)}</code> 297: </p> 298: ` : ''} 299: <div class="suggestion-metrics"> 300: <span class="metric-badge"> 301: Impact: ${suggestion.impact_score.toFixed(0)}% 302: </span> 303: <span class="metric-badge complexity-${suggestion.complexity}"> 304: ${suggestion.complexity} complexity 305: </span> 306: <span class="metric-badge risk-${suggestion.migration_risk}"> 307: ${suggestion.migration_risk} risk 308: </span> 309: ${suggestion.estimated_effort_hours != null ? ` 310: <span class="metric-badge"> 311: ~${suggestion.estimated_effort_hours}h effort 312: </span> 313: ` : ''} 314: </div> 315: ${suggestion.breaking_changes ? ` 316: <div class="warning-box"> 317: ⚠️ <strong>Breaking Change:</strong> This consolidation may require API changes 318: </div> 319: ` : ''} 320: </div> 321: </div> 322: `).join('')} 323: </div> 324: </section>`; For comprehensive scanning, use the JavaScript version with ast-grep. 319: 320: --- 321: 322: ## Configuration 323: 324: ### Customize Thresholds 325: 326: Edit scanner files to adjust sensitivity: 327: 328: **Root Directory Analyzer:** 329: ```javascript 330: // lib/scanners/root-directory-analyzer.js 331: this.maxRootFiles = 20; // Change to your preference 332: this.thresholds = { 333: pythonFiles: 3, // Warn if > 3 Python files in root 334: shellScripts: 3, 335: jsFiles: 3, 336: configFiles: 5, 337: dataFiles: 0 // Never allow data files in root 338: }; 339: ``` 340: 341: **Timeout Pattern Detector:** 342: ```javascript 343: // lib/scanners/timeout-pattern-detector.js 344: // Add custom patterns in scan() method 345: const customIssues = await this.findCustomPattern(repoPath); 346: ``` 347: 348: --- 349: 350: ## Real Results 351: 352: ### AnalyticsBot Dashboard (Nov 18, 2025) 353: 354: **Problem:** 355: - Dashboard spinning forever 356: - No error messages 357: - Required browser restart 358: 359: **Scan Results:** 360: ``` 361: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:261 362: Promise.race() in getAnalyticsSummary() without timeout 363: 364: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:469 365: Promise.race() in listProjects() without timeout 366: 367: 🟡 MEDIUM: src/hooks/useDashboardData.ts:46 368: setLoading(true) without finally block 369: ``` 370: 371: **Fixes Applied:** 372: 1. 45: } 46: export class RepositoryScanner { 47: private readonly repomixWorker: InstanceType<typeof RepomixWorker>; 48: constructor(options: RepositoryScannerOptions = {}) { 49: this.repomixWorker = new RepomixWorker({ 50: outputBaseDir: options.outputBaseDir ?? config.outputBaseDir, 51: codeBaseDir: options.codeBaseDir ?? config.codeBaseDir, 52: maxConcurrent: options.maxConcurrent ?? config.maxConcurrent 53: }); 54: } 55: async scanRepository(repoPath: string, scanConfig: ScanConfig = {}): Promise<RepositoryScanOutput> { 56: const startTime = Date.now(); 57: logStart(logger, 'repository scan', { repoPath }); 58: try { 59: await this.validateRepository(repoPath); 60: const repoInfo = await this.getRepositoryInfo(repoPath); 61: let metadata: RepomixMetadata = { totalFiles: 0, totalLines: 0, languages: [] }; 62: let repomixOutput: string | null = null; 63: try { 64: const repomixResult = await this.runRepomixScan(repoPath); 65: metadata = await this.parseRepomixOutput(repomixResult.outputFile as string); 66: repomixOutput = repomixResult.outputFile as string; 67: } catch (error) { 68: logger.warn({ error: (error as Error).message }, 'Repomix scan failed, using basic file discovery'); 69: const files = await this.listFiles(repoPath); 98: } catch (error) { 99: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Repository scan failed'); 100: throw new RepositoryScanError(`Failed to scan repository: ${(error as Error).message}`, { 101: cause: error 102: }); 103: } 104: } 105: async validateRepository(repoPath: string): Promise<void> { 106: try { 107: const stats = await fs.stat(repoPath); 108: if (!stats.isDirectory()) { 109: throw new Error('Path is not a directory'); 110: } 111: try { 112: await fs.access(path.join(repoPath, '.git')); 113: } catch { 114: logger.warn({ repoPath }, 'Not a git repository, proceeding anyway'); 115: } 116: } catch (error) { 117: throw new Error(`Invalid repository path: ${(error as Error).message}`); 129: try { 130: const gitDir = path.join(repoPath, '.git'); 131: await fs.access(gitDir); 132: try { 133: info.git_remote = execSync('git config --get remote.origin.url', { 134: cwd: repoPath, 135: encoding: 'utf-8' 136: }).trim(); 137: } catch { 138: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'No git remote found'); 139: } 140: try { 141: info.git_branch = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', { 142: cwd: repoPath, 143: encoding: 'utf-8' 144: }).trim(); 163: const job = this.repomixWorker.createRepomixJob(repoPath, relativePath); 164: return await this.repomixWorker.runJobHandler(job) as Record<string, unknown>; 165: } 166: async parseRepomixOutput(outputFile: string): Promise<RepomixMetadata> { 167: try { 168: const content = await fs.readFile(outputFile, 'utf-8'); 175: if (dirStructMatch) { 176: const structure = dirStructMatch[1]; 177: metadata.totalFiles = (structure.match(/\n\s+[^\s/]+\.[^\s/]+$/gm) ?? []).length; 178: } 179: const fileMatches = content.matchAll(/<file path="([^"]+)">/g); 180: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 181: '.js': 'javascript', 182: '.ts': 'typescript', 183: '.jsx': 'javascript', 184: '.tsx': 'typescript', 185: '.py': 'python', 186: '.go': 'go', 187: '.rs': 'rust', 188: '.java': 'java', 189: '.rb': 'ruby', 190: '.php': 'php' 191: }; 192: for (const match of fileMatches) { 193: metadata.totalFiles++; 194: const ext = path.extname(match[1]).toLowerCase(); 195: if (langMap[ext]) { 196: metadata.languages.add(langMap[ext]); 197: } 198: } 199: const linesMatch = content.match(/\n/g); 200: metadata.totalLines = linesMatch ? linesMatch.length : 0; 201: return { 202: totalFiles: metadata.totalFiles, 203: totalLines: metadata.totalLines, 204: languages: Array.from(metadata.languages) 205: }; 206: } catch (error) { 207: logger.warn({ outputFile, error }, 'Failed to parse repomix output'); 208: return { 209: totalFiles: 0, 210: totalLines: 0, 211: languages: [] 212: }; 213: } 214: } 215: async getFileMetadata(_repoPath: string, _scanConfig: ScanConfig): Promise<unknown[]> { 216: return []; 221: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 222: '.js': 'javascript', 223: '.ts': 'typescript', 224: '.jsx': 'javascript', 225: '.tsx': 'typescript', 226: '.py': 'python' 227: }; 228: async function walk(dir: string): Promise<void> { 229: const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true }); 230: for (const entry of entries) { 231: const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name); 232: if (entry.isDirectory()) { 233: if (ignoredDirs.has(entry.name)) { 234: continue; 235: } 236: await walk(fullPath); 237: } else { 238: if (filter.languages) { 239: const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase(); 240: if (filter.languages.includes(langMap[ext])) { 241: files.push(fullPath); 242: } 243: } else { 244: files.push(fullPath); 67: categorized: CategorizedFiles; 68: dependencies: Dependencies; 69: recommendations: Recommendation[]; 70: statistics: AnalysisStatistics; 71: } 72: type FileCategoryKey = keyof CategorizedFiles; 73: export class RootDirectoryAnalyzer { 74: private readonly logger: Pick<Console, 'info' | 'error'>; 75: private readonly maxRootFiles: number; 76: private readonly thresholds: { 77: pythonFiles: number; 78: shellScripts: number; 79: jsFiles: number; 80: configFiles: number; 81: dataFiles: number; 82: }; 83: constructor(options: RootDirectoryAnalyzerOptions = {}) { 84: this.logger = options.logger ?? console; 85: this.maxRootFiles = options.maxRootFiles ?? 20; 86: this.thresholds = { 87: pythonFiles: 3, 88: shellScripts: 3, 89: jsFiles: 3, 90: configFiles: 5, 91: dataFiles: 0 92: }; 93: } 94: async analyze(repoPath: string, _options: AnalyzeOptions = {}): Promise<RootDirectoryAnalysis> { 95: const startTime = Date.now(); 96: this.logger.info(`[RootDirectoryAnalyzer] Starting analysis: ${repoPath}`); 97: try { 98: const rootFiles = await this.getRootFiles(repoPath); 99: const categorized = this.categorizeFiles(rootFiles); 100: const dependencies = await this.analyzeImportDependencies(repoPath, categorized); 101: const recommendations = this.generateRecommendations(categorized, dependencies); 102: const reductionPotential = recommendations.reduce((sum, r) => sum + r.files.length, 0); 103: const statistics: AnalysisStatistics = { 104: total_root_files: rootFiles.length, 105: reduction_potential: reductionPotential, 106: final_root_files: rootFiles.length - reductionPotential, 107: reduction_percentage: rootFiles.length > 0 108: ? Math.round((reductionPotential / rootFiles.length) * 100) 109: : 0, 110: scan_duration_ms: Date.now() - startTime 111: }; 112: const result: RootDirectoryAnalysis = { 113: root_files: rootFiles, 114: categorized, 115: dependencies, 116: recommendations, 117: statistics 118: }; 119: this.logger.info( 120: `[RootDirectoryAnalyzer] Analysis complete: ${rootFiles.length} files, ${statistics.reduction_potential} can be moved (${statistics.reduction_percentage}% reduction)` 121: ); 121: return matches 122: .filter(match => { 123: const context = match.matched_text.toLowerCase(); 124: return !( 125: context.includes('timeout') || 126: context.includes('settimeout') || 127: context.includes('withtimeout') 128: ); 147: } 148: async findLoadingWithoutFinally(repoPath: string): Promise<TimeoutFinding[]> { 149: const pattern = 'setLoading(true)'; 150: const matches = await this.searchPattern(repoPath, pattern, { 151: language: 'typescript', 152: extensions: ['.ts', '.tsx', '.js', '.jsx'] 153: }); 154: return matches.map(match => ({ 155: ...match, 156: severity: 'medium' as const, 157: category: 'loading_without_finally', 158: message: 'setLoading(true) should be paired with finally block to ensure reset', 159: recommendation: 'Add finally block with setLoading(false)', 160: fix_example: ` 161: // Pattern: 162: try { 163: setLoading(true); 174: const matches = await this.searchPattern(repoPath, pattern, { 175: language: 'typescript', 176: extensions: ['.ts', '.tsx', '.js', '.jsx'] 177: }); 178: return matches 179: .filter(match => { 180: const body = match.matched_text; 181: return !( 182: body.includes('try') && 183: body.includes('catch') 184: ); 185: }) 186: .map(match => ({ 187: ...match, 188: severity: 'medium' as const, 189: category: 'async_no_error_handling', 190: message: 'Async function without try-catch - errors may be unhandled', 191: recommendation: 'Add try-catch block or propagate errors explicitly' 192: })); 246: if (!hasSafetyNet && fileMatches.some(m => m.matched_text.includes('true'))) { 247: findings.push({ 248: file_path: filePath, 249: line_start: fileMatches[0].line_start, 250: line_end: fileMatches[0].line_end, 251: matched_text: fileMatches[0].matched_text, 252: meta_variables: {}, 253: severity: 'low' as const, 254: category: 'missing_loading_safety_net', 255: message: 'Consider adding maximum loading timeout safety net', 256: recommendation: 'Add useEffect with timeout to force loading=false after max wait time', 257: fix_example: ` 258: // Add safety net in useDashboardData or similar hooks: 259: useEffect(() => { 260: if (!loading) return; 266: return () => clearTimeout(maxLoadingTimeout); 267: }, [loading]);` 268: }); 269: } 270: } 271: return findings; 272: } 273: async searchPattern(repoPath: string, pattern: string, options: SearchOptions = {}): Promise<PatternMatch[]> { 274: return new Promise<PatternMatch[]>((resolve, reject) => { 275: const args = [ 276: 'run', 277: '--pattern', pattern, 278: '--json' 279: ]; 280: if (options.language) { 281: args.push('--lang', options.language); 282: } 283: const proc: ChildProcess = spawn(this.astGrepBinary, args, { 284: cwd: repoPath, 285: timeout: 30000 286: }); 287: const output = captureProcessOutput(proc); '') as string, 299: meta_variables: (r['metaVars'] ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown> 300: })); 301: resolve(normalized); 302: } catch (error) { 303: this.logger.warn(`Failed to parse ast-grep output: ${(error as Error).message}`); 304: resolve([]); 305: } 306: } else { 307: reject(new Error(`ast-grep exited with code ${code}: ${stderr}`)); 308: } 309: }); 310: proc.on('error', (error: Error & { code?: string }) => { 311: if (error.code === 'ENOENT') { 312: reject(new Error('ast-grep (sg) not found. 'info'; 361: breakdown[severity] = (breakdown[severity] ?? 0) + 1; 362: } 363: return breakdown; 364: } 365: generateReport(findings: TimeoutFindings): string { 366: const lines = [ 367: '# Timeout Pattern Detection Report', 368: '', 369: `**Generated:** ${new Date().toISOString()}`, 370: `**Total Issues:** ${findings.statistics.total_issues}`, 371: `**Affected Files:** ${findings.statistics.affected_files}`, 372: `**Scan Duration:** ${findings.statistics.scan_duration_ms}ms`, 373: '', 374: '## Severity Breakdown', 375: '' 376: ]; 377: const severityEmoji: Record<string, string> = { 378: high: '🔴', 379: medium: '🟡', 380: low: '🟢', 381: info: '🔵' 382: }; 383: for (const [severity, count] of Object.entries(findings.statistics.severity_breakdown)) { 384: if (count > 0) { 385: const emoji = severityEmoji[severity] ?? 294: n = len(blocks) 295: groups = [] 296: used = set() 297: for i, block1 in enumerate(blocks): 298: if i in used: 299: continue 300: group = [block1] 301: similarities = [] 302: for j in range(i + groups.append((group, avg_similarity)) 324: else: 325: _stderr( 326: f"Warning: Group rejected by semantic validation: {[b.block_id for b in group]}" 327: ) 328: return groups 329: # SemanticWeights.EMPTY_SET_SIMILARITY 345: intersection = len(set1 & set2) 346: union = len(set1 | set2) 347: return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0 348: def _calculate_semantic_similarity( field(default_factory=set) 13: logical_operators: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set) 14: semantic_methods: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set) 15: def extract_semantic_features(source_code: str) source_code): 21: status_code = int(match.group(1)) 22: features.http_status_codes.add(status_code) 23: # Extract logical operators (===, !==, ==, !=, !, &&, ||) 24: # Order matters: match longer "=="), # Loose equality 30: (r"!\s*[^=]", "!"), # Logical NOT (followed by non-=) 31: (r"&&", "&&"), # Logical AND 32: (r"\|\|", "||"), # Logical OR 33: ] 34: for pattern, operator_name in operator_patterns: 35: if re.search(pattern, source_code): 36: features.logical_operators.add(operator_name) 37: # Extract semantic methods (Math.max, Math.min, console.log, etc.) 38: semantic_patterns = { 39: "Math.max": r"Math\.max\s*\(", 40: "Math.min": r"Math\.min\s*\(", 41: "Math.floor": r"Math\.floor\s*\(", 42: "Math.ceil": r"Math\.ceil\s*\(", 43: "Math.round": r"Math\.round\s*\(", 44: "console.log": r"console\.log\s*\(", 45: "console.error": r"console\.error\s*\(", 46: "console.warn": r"console\.warn\s*\(", 47: ".reverse": r"\.reverse\s*\(", 48: ".toUpperCase": r"\.toUpperCase\s*\(", 49: ".toLowerCase": r"\.toLowerCase\s*\(", 50: } 51: for method_name, pattern in semantic_patterns.items(): 52: if re.search(pattern, source_code): 53: features.semantic_methods.add(method_name) 54: return features 55: def normalize_code(source_code: str) -> str: 56: """ 57: Normalize code by removing variable-specific information. 58: This allows structural comparison by focusing on code structure 59: rather than specific names or values. 60: """ 61: if not source_code: 62: return "" 63: # Remove comments 64: normalized = re.sub( 65: r"//.*?$", "", source_code, flags=re.MULTILINE 66: ) # Single-line comments 67: normalized = re.sub( 68: r"/\*.*?\*/", "", normalized, flags=re.DOTALL 69: ) # Multi-line comments 70: # Normalize whitespace (collapse to single spaces) 71: normalized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", normalized) 72: # Normalize string literals (replace with placeholder) 73: normalized = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", "'STR'", normalized) 74: normalized = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '"STR"', normalized) 75: normalized = re.sub(r"`[^`]*`", "`STR`", normalized) 76: # Normalize numbers (replace with placeholder) 77: normalized = re.sub(r"\b\d+\b", "NUM", normalized) 78: # Semantic operator and method whitelist 79: # These methods have semantic meaning and should be preserved during normalization 80: SEMANTIC_METHODS = { 81: # Array functional methods (already preserved) 82: "map", 83: "filter", 84: "reduce", 85: "forEach", 86: "find", 87: "some", 88: "every", 89: "slice", 90: "splice", 91: "push", 92: "pop", 93: "shift", 94: "unshift", 95: "join", 96: "split", 97: "includes", 98: "indexOf", 99: # Object methods (already preserved) 100: "get", 101: "set", 102: "has", 103: "delete", 104: "keys", 105: "values", 106: "entries", 107: # Async patterns (already preserved) 108: "then", 109: "catch", 110: "finally", 111: "async", 112: "await", 113: # Array transformations (already preserved) 114: "reverse", 115: "sort", 116: "concat", 117: # NEW: Math operations (opposite semantics) 118: "max", 119: "min", 120: "abs", 121: "floor", 122: "ceil", 123: "round", 124: # NEW: String operations (semantic meaning) 125: "trim", 126: "toLowerCase", 127: "toUpperCase", 128: "replace", 129: # NEW: HTTP/API methods (semantic differences) 130: "status", 131: "json", 132: "send", 133: "redirect", 134: # NEW: Properties with semantic value 135: "length", 136: "name", 137: "value", 138: "id", 139: "type", 140: } 141: # Preserve important objects 142: SEMANTIC_OBJECTS = { 143: "Math", 144: "Object", 145: "Array", 146: "String", 147: "Number", 148: "Boolean", 149: "console", 150: "process", 151: "JSON", 152: "Date", 153: "Promise", 154: } 155: # Build a pattern that matches identifiers but NOT important methods/objects 156: # First, mark important objects for preservation (Math, Object, etc.) 157: for obj in SEMANTIC_OBJECTS: 158: normalized = re.sub( 159: rf"\b{obj}\b", f"__PRESERVE_OBJ_{obj.upper()}__", normalized 160: ) 161: # Second, mark important methods so they're not replaced 162: for method in SEMANTIC_METHODS: 163: normalized = re.sub( 164: rf"\b{method}\b", f"__PRESERVE_{method.upper()}__", normalized 165: ) 166: normalized = re.sub(r"\b[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\b", "var", normalized) 167: normalized = re.sub(r"\b[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*\b", "CONST", normalized) 168: for obj in SEMANTIC_OBJECTS: 169: normalized = normalized.replace(f"__PRESERVE_OBJ_{obj.upper()}__", obj) 170: for method in SEMANTIC_METHODS: 171: normalized = normalized.replace(f"__PRESERVE_{method.upper()}__", method) 172: normalized = re.sub(r"\s*([(){}[\];,.])\s*", r"\1", normalized) 173: normalized = re.sub(r"\s*(=>|===?|!==?|[+\-*/%<>=&|])\s*", r" \1 ", normalized) 174: # Collapse multiple spaces 175: normalized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", normalized) 176: # Trim 177: normalized = normalized.strip() 178: return normalized 179: def calculate_ast_hash(source_code: str) -> str: 180: """ 181: Calculate a hash based on normalized code structure. 182: This creates a structural fingerprint that's the same for 183: code with the same structure but different variable names. 184: """ 185: normalized = normalize_code(source_code) 186: return hashlib.sha256(normalized.encode()).hexdigest() 187: def calculate_levenshtein_similarity(str1: str, str2: str) -> float: 188: """ 189: Calculate similarity using Levenshtein distance via SequenceMatcher. 190: Returns a similarity ratio between 0.0 and 1.0. 191: """ 192: if not str1 or not str2: 193: return 0.0 194: return SequenceMatcher(None, str1, str2).ratio() 195: def extract_logical_operators(source_code: str) -> set: 196: """ 197: Extract logical operators from source code for semantic comparison. 198: Returns: 199: Set of logical operators found (e.g., {'===', '!=='}) 200: """ 201: operators = set() 202: # Find all logical operators 203: # Check compound operators first (order matters) 204: if "!==" in source_code: 205: operators.add("!==") 206: if "===" in source_code: 207: operators.add("===") 208: if "!=" in source_code and "!==" not in source_code: 209: operators.add("!=") 210: if "==" in source_code and "===" not in source_code: 211: operators.add("==") 212: # Match standalone ! (not part of !== or !=) 213: if re.search(r"(?<![!=])!(?![=])", source_code): 214: operators.add("!") 215: return operators 216: def extract_http_status_codes(source_code: str) -> set: 217: """ 218: Extract HTTP status codes from response patterns. 219: Returns: 220: Set of HTTP status codes found in response patterns 221: """ 222: status_codes = set() 223: # Pattern: res.status(200), response.status(404), etc. 224: pattern = r"(?:res|response)\.status\((\d{3})\)" 225: matches = re.finditer(pattern, source_code) 226: for match in matches: 227: status_codes.add(int(match.group(1))) 228: return status_codes 229: def extract_semantic_methods(source_code: str) -> set: 230: """ 231: Extract critical semantic methods that indicate opposite or different behavior. 232: Returns: 233: Set of semantic methods found (e.g., {'Math.max', 'Math.min'}) 234: """ 235: methods = set() 236: # Critical Math methods with opposite semantics 237: math_opposites = ["max", "min", "floor", "ceil"] 238: for method in math_opposites: 239: if f"Math.{method}" in source_code: 240: methods.add(f"Math.{method}") 241: return methods 242: def extract_method_chain(source_code: str) -> list: 243: """ 244: Extract method chain structure from code. 245: Returns: 246: List of methods in chain order (e.g., ['filter', 'map', 'reverse']) 247: """ 248: # Pattern: .method1().method2().method3() 249: # Handles both arr.filter().map() and arr.filter(fn).map(fn) 250: chains = [] 251: # Find chained method calls 252: # Pattern: .method_name( ... ).method_name( ... ) 253: pattern = r"\.([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\s*\(" 254: matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, source_code)) 255: if not matches: 256: return [] 257: # Build chains by tracking consecutive method calls 258: current_chain = [] 259: last_pos = -1 260: for i, match in enumerate(matches): 261: method_name = match.group(1) 262: # Check if this is part of a chain (close to previous match) 263: # Allow up to METHOD_CHAIN_MAX_GAP characters between methods for complex arguments 264: if ( 265: current_chain 266: and match.start() - last_pos > ExtractionDefaults.METHOD_CHAIN_MAX_GAP 267: ): 268: # Too far apart, start a new chain 269: if len(current_chain) > 1: # Only save actual chains (2+ methods) 270: chains.append(current_chain) 271: current_chain = [method_name] 272: else: 273: current_chain.append(method_name) 274: # Find end of this method call (rough approximation) 275: # Look for closing paren or next method 276: if i < len(matches) - 1: 277: last_pos = matches[i + 1].start() 278: else: 279: last_pos = len(source_code) 280: # Add the last chain if it has multiple methods 281: if len(current_chain) > 1: 282: chains.append(current_chain) 283: # Return longest chain (most significant) 284: return max(chains, key=len) if chains else [] 285: def compare_method_chains(code1: str, code2: str) -> float: 286: """ 287: Compare method chain structure between two code blocks. 288: Returns: 289: Similarity score 0.0-1.0 based on chain overlap 290: """ 291: chain1 = extract_method_chain(code1) 292: chain2 = extract_method_chain(code2) 293: if not chain1 and not chain2: 294: return 1.0 # No chains in either 295: if not chain1 or not chain2: 296: return ( 297: StructuralDefaults.CHAIN_MISSING_SIMILARITY 298: ) # One has chain, other doesn't 299: # Exact match 300: if chain1 == chain2: 301: return 1.0 302: # Check if one is a subset of the other (additional operation) 303: if len(chain1) != len(chain2): 304: # Different length chains → likely different behavior 305: # e.g., [filter, map] vs [filter, map, reverse] 306: # Check if shorter is prefix of longer 307: shorter = chain1 if len(chain1) < len(chain2) else chain2 308: longer = chain1 if len(chain1) > len(chain2) else chain2 309: if longer[: len(shorter)] == shorter: 310: # One is extension of other → partial match 311: # Similarity based on overlap ratio 312: return len(shorter) / len(longer) 313: else: 314: # Different chains entirely 315: return 0.0 316: # Same length but different methods → check overlap 317: overlap = sum(1 for m1, m2 in zip(chain1, chain2) if m1 == m2) 318: return overlap / len(chain1) 319: def calculate_semantic_penalty( 320: features1: SemanticFeatures, features2: SemanticFeatures 321: ) -> float: 322: """ 323: Calculate combined semantic penalty based on extracted features. 324: Penalties are multiplicative - each mismatch reduces similarity: 325: - HTTP status codes: STATUS_CODE_PENALTY multiplier 326: - Logical operators: LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY multiplier 327: - Semantic methods: SEMANTIC_METHOD_PENALTY multiplier 328: Args: 329: features1: Semantic features from first code block 330: features2: Semantic features from second code block 331: Returns: 332: Penalty multiplier (0.0-1.0). Returns 1.0 if no penalties apply. 333: Example: 334: Different status codes + different operators (=== vs !==) 335: = STATUS_CODE_PENALTY * LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY final similarity 336: """ 337: penalty = 1.0 338: # Penalty 1: HTTP Status Code Mismatch (30% penalty) 339: if features1.http_status_codes and features2.http_status_codes: 340: if features1.http_status_codes != features2.http_status_codes: 341: # Different status codes indicate different semantic intent 342: # (e.g., 200 OK vs 201 Created vs 404 Not Found) 343: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.STATUS_CODE_PENALTY 344: # Penalty 2: Logical Operator Mismatch (20% penalty) 345: if features1.logical_operators and features2.logical_operators: 346: if features1.logical_operators != features2.logical_operators: 347: # Different logical operators indicate different boolean logic 348: # (e.g., === vs !==, && vs ||) 349: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY 350: # Penalty 3: Semantic Method Mismatch (25% penalty) 351: if features1.semantic_methods and features2.semantic_methods: 352: if features1.semantic_methods != features2.semantic_methods: 353: # Different semantic methods indicate different operations 354: # (e.g., Math.max vs Math.min, toUpperCase vs toLowerCase) 355: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.SEMANTIC_METHOD_PENALTY 356: return penalty 357: def calculate_structural_similarity( 358: code1: str, 359: code2: str, 360: threshold: float = StructuralDefaults.DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, 361: ) -> Tuple[float, str]: 362: """ 363: Calculate structural similarity between two code blocks using unified penalty system. 364: Priority: Structural Similarity (structural layer of multi-layer algorithm) 365: Returns: 366: (similarity_score, method) 367: - similarity_score: 0.0 to 1.0 368: - method: 'exact', 'structural', or 'different' 369: Algorithm (multi-phase flow): 370: - Exact match: Compare hashes → 1.0 similarity 371: - Extract semantic features from ORIGINAL code (BEFORE normalization) 372: - Normalize code and calculate base structural similarity 373: - Apply unified semantic penalties using original features 374: - Return final similarity score and method 375: """ 376: if not code1 or not code2: 377: return 0.0, "different" 378: hash1 = hashlib.sha256(code1.encode()).hexdigest() 379: hash2 = hashlib.sha256(code2.encode()).hexdigest() 380: if hash1 == hash2: 381: return 1.0, "exact" 382: features1 = safeErrorMessage(error: unknown, fallback = 'Unknown error'): string { 27: if (error === null || error === undefined) { 28: return fallback; 29: } 30: if (error instanceof Error) { 31: return error.message || fallback; 32: } 33: if (typeof error === 'object' && 'message' in error && typeof (error as { message: unknown }).message === 'string') { 34: return (error as { message: string }).message || fallback; 35: } 36: if (typeof error === 'string') { 37: return error || fallback; 38: } 39: try { 40: const stringified = String(error); 41: if (stringified === '[object Object]') { 42: null || error === undefined) { 51: return undefined; 52: } 53: if (error instanceof Error && error.stack) { 54: return error.stack; 55: } 56: if (typeof error === 'object' && 'stack' in error && fallbackMessage), 68: stack: safeErrorStack(error), 69: type: getErrorType(error), 70: isError: error instanceof Error, 71: metadata 72: }; 73: if (error instanceof Error && error.name) { 74: errorObj.name = error.name; 75: } 76: if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error) { 77: errorObj.code = (error as { code: unknown }).code; 78: } 79: return errorObj; 80: } 81: function getErrorType(error: unknown): string { 82: if (error === null || error === undefined) { 83: return error === null ? 'null' : 'undefined'; 84: } 85: if (error instanceof Error) { 86: return error.constructor.name || 'Error'; 87: } 88: return typeof error; 89: } 90: export function isErrorLike(value: unknown): boolean { 91: if (value instanceof Error) { 92: return true; 93: } 94: if (value && typeof value === 'object') { 95: return 'message' in value || 'stack' in value; 96: } 97: return false; 98: } 99: export function serializeError(error: unknown, includeStack = true): SerializedError { 100: const serialized: SerializedError = { 101: message: safeErrorMessage(error), 102: type: getErrorType(error) 103: }; 104: if (error instanceof Error) { 105: serialized.name = error.name; 106: if (includeStack && error.stack) { 107: serialized.stack = error.stack; 108: } 109: if (error.cause) { 110: serialized.cause = serializeError(error.cause, includeStack); 111: } 112: } 113: if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error) { 114: serialized.code = (error as { code: unknown }).code; 115: } 116: return serialized; 117: } 118: export function formatErrorMessage(error: unknown, options: FormatErrorOptions = {}): string { 119: const { 120: includeType = false, 121: includeCode = false 122: } = options; 123: const message = safeErrorMessage(error); 124: const parts: string[] = []; 125: if (includeCode && error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error) { 126: parts.push(`[${(error as { code: unknown }).code}]`); 127: } else if (includeType) { 128: parts.push(`[${getErrorType(error)}]`); 129: } 130: parts.push(message); 131: return parts.join(' '); 132: } 133: export function combineErrors(errors: unknown[], separator = '; '): string { 134: if (!Array.isArray(errors) || errors.length === 0) { 135: return 'Unknown error'; 136: } 137: return errors 138: .map(error => safeErrorMessage(error)) 139: .filter(msg => msg && msg !== 'Unknown error') 140: .join(separator); type CacheSeverity = 'healthy' | 'warning' | 'critical' | 'error'; 10: export interface CacheHealthStatus { 11: healthy: boolean; 12: cacheAgeMs: number; 13: cacheAgeHours: number; 14: cacheAgeMinutes: number; 15: lastModified: string | null; 16: usingFallback: boolean; 17: fileCount: number; 18: severity: CacheSeverity; 19: newestFile?: string; 20: error?: string; 21: errorCode?: string; 22: } 23: export interface DopplerHealthMonitorOptions { 24: cacheDir?: string; 25: maxCacheAge?: number; 26: warningThreshold?: number; 27: } 28: export class DopplerHealthMonitor { 29: private cacheDir: string; 30: private maxCacheAge: number; 31: private warningThreshold: number; 32: private monitoringInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null; 33: constructor(options: DopplerHealthMonitorOptions = {}) { 34: this.cacheDir = options.cacheDir ?? path.join(os.homedir(), '.doppler', 'fallback'); 35: this.maxCacheAge = options.maxCacheAge ?? CACHE.MAX_AGE_MS; 36: this.warningThreshold = options.warningThreshold ?? CACHE.WARNING_THRESHOLD_MS; 37: this.monitoringInterval = null; 38: } 39: async checkCacheHealth(): Promise<CacheHealthStatus> { 40: try { 41: const dirStats = await fs.stat(this.cacheDir); 42: if (!dirStats.isDirectory()) { 43: throw new Error('Fallback path exists but is not a directory'); 44: } 45: const files = await fs.readdir(this.cacheDir); 46: const secretFiles = files.filter(f => f.startsWith('.secrets-') && f.endsWith('.json')); 47: if (secretFiles.length === 0) { 48: logger.warn({ cacheDir: this.cacheDir }, 'Doppler fallback directory exists but contains no secret files'); 49: return { 50: healthy: false, 51: cacheAgeMs: 0, 52: cacheAgeHours: 0, 53: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 54: lastModified: null, 55: usingFallback: false, 56: fileCount: 0, 57: severity: 'warning' 58: }; 59: } 60: let newestFile: string | null = null; 61: let newestMtime = 0; 62: for (const file of secretFiles) { 63: const filePath = path.join(this.cacheDir, file); 64: const stats = await fs.stat(filePath); 65: if (stats.mtime.getTime() > newestMtime) { 66: newestMtime = stats.mtime.getTime(); 67: newestFile = file; 68: } 69: } 70: const cacheAge = Date.now() - newestMtime; 71: const cacheAgeHours = Math.floor(cacheAge / TIME_MS.HOUR); 72: const cacheAgeMinutes = Math.floor((cacheAge % TIME_MS.HOUR) / TIME_MS.MINUTE); 73: const status: CacheHealthStatus = { 74: healthy: cacheAge <= this.maxCacheAge, 75: cacheAgeMs: cacheAge, 76: cacheAgeHours, 77: cacheAgeMinutes, 78: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 79: newestFile: newestFile ?? undefined, 80: fileCount: secretFiles.length, 81: usingFallback: true, 82: severity: this.getSeverity(cacheAge) 83: }; 84: if (cacheAge > this.maxCacheAge) { 85: logger.error({ 86: cacheAgeHours, 87: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime), 88: newestFile, 89: fileCount: secretFiles.length 90: }, 'Doppler cache is critically stale - secrets may be outdated'); 91: Sentry.captureMessage('Doppler cache critically stale', { 92: level: 'error', 93: extra: { 94: cacheAgeHours, 95: cacheAgeMinutes, 96: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 97: threshold: this.maxCacheAge / TIME_MS.HOUR, 98: newestFile, 99: fileCount: secretFiles.length 100: }, 101: tags: { 102: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 103: severity: 'critical' 104: } 105: }); 106: } else if (cacheAge > this.warningThreshold) { 107: logger.warn({ 108: cacheAgeHours, 109: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime), 110: newestFile 111: }, 'Doppler cache is aging - approaching staleness threshold'); 112: Sentry.captureMessage('Doppler cache aging', { 113: level: 'warning', 114: extra: { 115: cacheAgeHours, 116: cacheAgeMinutes, 117: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 118: threshold: this.warningThreshold / TIME_MS.HOUR, 119: newestFile, 120: fileCount: secretFiles.length 121: }, 122: tags: { 123: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 124: severity: 'warning' 125: } 126: }); 127: } else { 128: logger.debug({ 129: cacheAgeHours, 130: cacheAgeMinutes, 131: fileCount: secretFiles.length 132: }, 'Doppler cache health check - within thresholds'); 133: } 134: return status; 135: } catch (error) { 136: const nodeError = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException; 137: if (nodeError.code === 'ENOENT') { 138: logger.debug({ cacheDir: this.cacheDir }, 'No Doppler fallback cache found - likely using live API only'); 139: return { 140: healthy: true, 141: cacheAgeMs: 0, 142: cacheAgeHours: 0, 143: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 144: lastModified: null, 145: usingFallback: false, 146: fileCount: 0, 147: severity: 'healthy' 148: }; 149: } 150: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to check Doppler cache health', { cacheDir: this.cacheDir }); 151: Sentry.captureException(error, { 152: tags: { 153: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 154: operation: 'check-cache-health' 155: } 156: }); 157: return { 158: healthy: false, 159: error: nodeError.message, 160: errorCode: nodeError.code, 161: severity: 'error', 162: cacheAgeMs: 0, 163: cacheAgeHours: 0, 164: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 165: lastModified: null, 166: usingFallback: false, 167: fileCount: 0 168: }; 169: } 170: } 171: getSeverity(cacheAge: number): CacheSeverity { 172: if (cacheAge > this.maxCacheAge) { 173: return 'critical'; 174: } else if (cacheAge > this.warningThreshold) { 175: return 'warning'; 176: } else { 177: return 'healthy'; 178: } 179: } 180: async startMonitoring(intervalMinutes = 15): Promise<CacheHealthStatus> { 181: if (this.monitoringInterval) { 182: logger.warn('Monitoring already started, stopping previous interval'); 183: this.stopMonitoring(); 184: } 185: logger.info({ 186: intervalMinutes, 187: maxCacheAgeHours: this.maxCacheAge / TIME_MS.HOUR, 188: warningThresholdHours: this.warningThreshold / TIME_MS.HOUR 189: }, 'Starting Doppler health monitoring'); 190: const initialHealth = await this.checkCacheHealth(); 191: logger.info({ initialHealth }, 'Initial Doppler health check complete'); 192: this.monitoringInterval = setInterval(async () => { 193: await this.checkCacheHealth(); 194: }, intervalMinutes * TIME_MS.MINUTE); 195: return initialHealth; 196: } 197: stopMonitoring(): void { 198: if (this.monitoringInterval) { 199: clearInterval(this.monitoringInterval); 200: this.monitoringInterval = null; 315: const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / TIME_MS.SECOND; 316: logger.info({ 317: repoPath, 318: duration, 319: blocks: pythonResult.metrics?.total_code_blocks || 0, 320: groups: pythonResult.metrics?.total_duplicate_groups || 0, 321: suggestions: pythonResult.metrics?.total_suggestions || 0 322: }, 'Repository scan completed successfully'); error?: Error } { 355: if (code === 0) return this._handleSuccess(stdout, stderr); 356: if (code === null) return this._handleSignalTermination(signal, stdout, stderr); 357: return this._handleError(code, stderr); 358: } 359: private _handleSuccess( 360: stdout: string, 361: stderr: string 362: ): { success: boolean; data?: PythonPipelineOutput; error?: Error } { 363: try { 364: const result: PythonPipelineOutput = JSON.parse(stdout); 365: return { success: true, data: result }; 366: } catch (error) { 367: logger.error( 368: { stdout: stdout.slice(-500), stderr: stderr.slice(-500) }, 369: 'Failed to parse Python pipeline output' 370: ); 371: return { 372: success: false, 373: error: new Error(`Failed to parse Python output: ${(error as Error).message}`) 374: }; 375: } 376: } 377: private _handleSignalTermination( 378: signal: string | null, 379: stdout: string, 380: stderr: string 381: ): { success: boolean; data?: PythonPipelineOutput; error?: Error } { 382: if (stdout.trim()) { 383: try { 384: const result: PythonPipelineOutput = JSON.parse(stdout); 385: logger.warn( 386: { signal, stderrTail: stderr.slice(-200) }, 387: `Python pipeline was killed by signal ${signal} but produced valid output - treating as success` 388: ); 389: return { success: true, data: result }; 390: } catch { 391: } 392: } 393: const timeoutMsg = signal === 'SIGTERM' 394: ? ' (likely due to timeout - consider increasing timeout or optimizing the scan)' 395: : ''; 396: logger.error({ signal, stderrTail: stderr.slice(-500) }, `Python pipeline killed by signal: ${signal}`); 397: return { 398: success: false, 399: error: new Error( 400: `Python pipeline was killed by signal ${signal}${timeoutMsg}\n` + 401: `stderr (last 200 chars): ${stderr.slice(-200)}` 402: ) 403: }; 404: } 405: /** 406: * Handle error exit (non-zero code) 407: */ 408: private _handleError( 409: code: number, 410: stderr: string 411: ): { success: boolean; data?: PythonPipelineOutput; error?: Error } { 412: logger.error({ code, stderrTail: stderr.slice(-500) }, 'Python pipeline failed'); 413: return { 414: success: false, 415: error: new Error(`Python pipeline exited with code ${code}: ${stderr.slice(-500)}`) 416: }; 417: } 418: private _calculateTimeout(data: PythonPipelineInput): number { 419: const baseTimeout = TIMEOUTS.PYTHON_PIPELINE_BASE_MS; 420: const patternCount = data.pattern_matches?.length ?? 0; 421: const fileCount = data.repository_info?.totalFiles ?? 0; 422: const dynamicTimeout = baseTimeout + 423: (patternCount * TIMEOUTS.PYTHON_PIPELINE_PER_PATTERN_MS) + 424: (fileCount * TIMEOUTS.PYTHON_PIPELINE_PER_FILE_MS); 425: logger.debug({ 426: baseTimeout, 427: patternCount, 428: fileCount, 429: dynamicTimeout 430: }, 'Calculated dynamic timeout for Python pipeline'); 431: return dynamicTimeout; 432: } 433: private async runPythonPipeline(data: PythonPipelineInput): Promise<PythonPipelineOutput> { 434: this._detectPythonPath(); 435: return new Promise<PythonPipelineOutput>((resolve, reject) => { 436: logger.debug('Launching Python extraction pipeline'); 437: const timeoutMs = this._calculateTimeout(data); 438: const proc: ChildProcess = spawn(this.pythonPath!, [this.extractorScript], { 439: timeout: timeoutMs, 440: }); 441: let stdout = ''; 442: let stderr = ''; 443: // Send input data via stdin 444: const jsonData = JSON.stringify(data); 445: logger.debug({ 446: patternMatchCount: data.pattern_matches?.length, 447: repoPath: data.repository_info?.path 448: }, 'Sending data to Python pipeline'); 449: proc.stdin?.write(jsonData); 450: proc.stdin?.end(); 451: proc.stdout?.on('data', (data: Buffer) => { 452: stdout += data.toString(); 453: }); 454: proc.stderr?.on('data', (data: Buffer) => { 455: const stderrText = data.toString(); 456: stderr += stderrText; 457: if (stderrText.includes('Warning:')) { 458: logger.warn({ stderr: stderrText }, 'Python pipeline warning'); 459: } else { 460: logger.debug({ stderr: stderrText }, 'Python pipeline stderr'); 461: } 462: }); 463: proc.on('close', (code: number | null, signal: string | null) => { 464: const result = this._handleProcessClose(code, signal, stdout, stderr); 465: if (result.success) { resolve(result.data!); } else { reject(result.error!); } 466: }); 467: proc.on('error', (error: Error & { code?: string }) => { 468: if (error.code === 'ENOENT') { 469: reject(new Error(`Python not found at: ${this.pythonPath}`)); 470: } else { 471: reject(error); 472: } 473: }); 474: }); 475: } 476: async generateReports(scanResult: ScanResult, options: ReportOptions = {}): Promise<ReportPaths> { 477: const repoInfo = scanResult.repository_info || {} as RepositoryInfo; 478: const repoName = repoInfo.name || 'scan'; 479: const isInterProject = scanResult.scan_type === 'inter-project'; 480: const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, -5); 481: const baseName = options.baseName || `${repoName}-${timestamp}`; 482: const outputDir = options.outputDir || this.outputDir; 483: await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true }); 484: const reportPaths: ReportPaths = {}; 'Inter-Project Duplicate Detection Report' 492: : `Duplicate Detection Report - ${repoName}`) 493: }); 494: reportPaths.html = htmlPath; 495: logger.info({ path: htmlPath }, 'HTML report generated'); 496: } 497: if (options.markdown !== false) { 498: logger.info({ outputDir }, 'Generating Markdown report'); 499: const markdownPath = path.join(outputDir, `${baseName}.md`); 500: await MarkdownReportGenerator.saveReport(scanResult as unknown as ReportScanResult, markdownPath, { 501: includeDetails: options.includeDetails !== false, 502: maxDuplicates: options.maxDuplicates || 10, 503: maxSuggestions: options.maxSuggestions || 10 504: }); 505: reportPaths.markdown = markdownPath; 506: logger.info({ path: markdownPath }, 'Markdown report generated'); 507: } 508: if (options.summary !== false) { 509: logger.info({ outputDir }, 'Generating summary'); 510: const summaryPath = path.join(outputDir, `${baseName}-summary.md`); 511: await MarkdownReportGenerator.saveSummary(scanResult as unknown as ReportScanResult, summaryPath); 512: reportPaths.summary = summaryPath; 513: logger.info({ path: summaryPath }, 'Summary generated'); 514: } 515: return reportPaths; 516: } catch (error) { 517: logger.error({ error }, 'Report generation failed'); 518: throw new ScanError(`Report generation failed: ${(error as Error).message}`, { 519: cause: error 520: }); 521: } 522: } 523: async scanMultipleRepositories( 524: repoPaths: string[], 525: scanConfig: ScanConfig = {} 526: ): Promise<MultiRepositoryScanResult> { 527: logStart(logger, 'multi-repository scan', { count: repoPaths.length }); 528: const interProjectScanner = new InterProjectScanner({ 529: orchestrator: { 530: scanner: {}, 531: detector: {}, 532: pythonPath: this.pythonPath, 533: extractorScript: this.extractorScript, 534: outputDir: this.outputDir, 535: autoGenerateReports: this.autoGenerateReports, 536: reports: this.reportConfig 537: }, 538: outputDir: this.outputDir 539: }); 540: try { 541: const interProjectResult = await interProjectScanner.scanRepositories(repoPaths, scanConfig) as unknown as { 542: repository_scans: Array<{ error?: string; repository_path: string; scan_result?: ScanResult }>; 568: } catch (error) { 569: logger.error({ error }, 'Multi-repository scan failed'); 570: logger.warn('Falling back to individual repository scans without cross-repository analysis'); 571: const results: Array<ScanResult | { error: string; repository_path: string }> = []; 572: for (const repoPath of repoPaths) { 573: try { 574: const result = await this.scanRepository(repoPath, scanConfig); 575: results.push(result); 576: } catch (scanError) { 577: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: scanError }, 'Repository scan failed, continuing'); 578: results.push({ 579: error: (scanError as Error).message, 580: repository_path: repoPath 581: }); 582: } 583: } 584: return { 585: repositories: results, 586: total_scanned: repoPaths.length, 587: successful: results.filter(r => !('error' in r)).length, 588: failed: results.filter(r => 'error' in r).length, 589: scan_type: 'single-project' 590: };
