599: } 600: this.name = 'ScanError'; 601: } 602: } 1: # Universal Repository Cleanup Script 2: 3: **Location:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 4: **Created:** 2025-11-17 5: **Purpose:** Clean up common bloat from any repository 6: 7: ## Quick Start 8: 9: ```bash 10: # Clean current directory 11: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 12: 13: # Clean specific directory 14: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 15: 16: # Clean a project 17: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 18: ``` 19: 20: ## What It Does 21: 22: This script automatically detects and removes common bloat from repositories: 23: 24: ### 1. Python Virtual Environments 25: Removes directories matching common venv patterns: 26: - `venv/`, `.venv/`, `env/`, `.env/` 27: - `virtualenv/`, `*.venv/` 28: - Custom patterns (e.g., `personal_site/`) 29: 30: ### 2. Temporary/Cache Files 31: Removes system and editor temporary files: 32: - `.DS_Store` (macOS) 33: - `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.pyo` (Python) 34: - `*.swp`, `*.swo`, `*~` (Vim/editor) 35: - `Thumbs.db`, `desktop.ini` (Windows) 36: 37: ### 3. Output/Generated Files 38: Removes generated output files: 39: - `repomix-output.xml` (nested, keeps root) 40: - `*.log`, `npm-debug.log*` 41: - `yarn-debug.log*`, `yarn-error.log*` 42: 43: ### 4. Build Artifacts 44: Removes common build directories: 45: - `.jekyll-cache`, `.sass-cache`, `.bundle` (Jekyll) 46: - `dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `.output/` (Build outputs) 47: - `.next/`, `.nuxt/` (Framework caches) 48: - `node_modules/.cache/` (NPM cache) 49: - `target/`, `.gradle/` (Java/Gradle) 50: 51: ### 5. Redundant Directories 52: Removes common redundant directories: 53: - `drafts/`, `temp/`, `tmp/` 54: - `backup/`, `backups/`, `old/` 55: - `archive/`, `deprecated/` 56: 57: ## Features 58: 59: ✅ **Safe & Interactive** 60: - Preview what will be removed before deletion 61: - Shows sizes and counts 62: - Requires explicit "yes" confirmation 63: 64: ✅ **Intelligent Scanning** 65: - Skips `node_modules/` when looking for venvs 66: - Preserves root-level `repomix-output.xml` 67: - Detects `.gitignore` entries 68: 69: ✅ **Universal Compatibility** 70: - Works on any repository type 71: - Configurable patterns 72: - Language agnostic 73: 74: ✅ **Visual Feedback** 75: - Color-coded output (green/yellow/red/blue) 76: - Progress indicators (✓ ✗ ⚠ →) 77: - Size calculations for removed items 78: 79: ## Usage Examples 80: 81: ### Example 1: Personal Website 82: ```bash 83: cd ~/projects/PersonalSite 84: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 85: ``` 86: 87: **Output:** 88: ``` 89: ======================================== 90: Repository Cleanup Preview 91: ======================================== 92: 93: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/PersonalSite 94: Current Size: 616M 95: 96: Python Virtual Environments (2 found): 97: - personal_site (40M) 98: - utils/numpy (42M) 99: 100: Temporary/Cache Files (3 found): 101: - 3 files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, .swp, etc.) 102: 103: Build Artifacts (2 found): 104: - .jekyll-cache (5M) 105: - dist (123K) 106: 107: Total items to remove: 7 108: 109: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): 110: ``` 111: 112: ### Example 2: React Project 113: ```bash 114: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/my-react-app 115: ``` 116: 117: Cleans: 118: - `.next/` cache 119: - `dist/` build output 120: - `.DS_Store` files 121: - `npm-debug.log` files 122: 123: ### Example 3: Python Project 124: ```bash 125: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/django-app 126: ``` 127: 128: Cleans: 129: - `venv/`, `.venv/` directories 130: - `__pycache__/` directories 131: - `*.pyc` files 132: - `*.log` files 133: 134: ## Customization 135: 136: Edit the script to customize what gets cleaned: 137: 138: ```bash 139: # Add custom venv patterns 140: VENV_PATTERNS=( 141: "venv" 142: ".venv" 143: "your_custom_venv_name" # Add here 144: ) 145: 146: # Add custom build artifacts 147: BUILD_ARTIFACTS=( 148: "dist" 149: "build" 150: "your_build_dir" # Add here 151: ) 152: 153: # Add custom redundant directories 154: REDUNDANT_DIRS=( 155: "drafts" 156: "temp" 157: "your_backup_dir" # Add here 158: ) 159: ``` 160: 161: ## Safety Features 162: 163: ### Preview Before Deletion 164: Always shows what will be removed with sizes before asking for confirmation 165: 166: ### No Accidental Deletions 167: - Requires explicit "yes" to proceed 168: - Exit codes on errors (`set -e`) 169: - Validates directory exists 170: 171: ### Preserves Important Files 172: - Skips `node_modules/` when scanning 173: - Keeps root-level `repomix-output.xml` 174: - Respects `.gitignore` patterns 175: 176: ### Error Handling 177: - Validates target directory exists 178: - Handles missing files gracefully 179: - Reports errors clearly 180: 181: ## Output Format 182: 183: ### Summary Report 184: ``` 185: ======================================== 186: Cleanup Summary 187: ======================================== 188: 189: Cleanup completed successfully! 190: 191: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/repo 192: Final Size: 150M 193: 194: Cleaned up: 195: ✓ Python virtual environments 196: ✓ Temporary/cache files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, etc.) 197: ✓ Output/generated files (logs, repomix files, etc.) 198: ✓ Build artifacts (.jekyll-cache, dist/, etc.) 199: ✓ Redundant directories (drafts/, temp/, backup/, etc.) 200: ``` 201: 202: ### Recommendations 203: ``` 204: ======================================== 205: Recommendations 206: ======================================== 207: 208: Consider adding these patterns to .gitignore if not already present: 209: 210: # Python 211: venv/ 212: .venv/ 213: *.pyc 214: __pycache__/ 215: 216: # System files 217: .DS_Store 218: Thumbs.db 219: 220: # Build artifacts 221: dist/ 222: build/ 223: *.log 224: ``` 225: 226: ## Common Use Cases 227: 228: ### 1. Before Committing 229: Clean up before committing to ensure no build artifacts or venvs are added: 230: ```bash 231: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 232: git status 233: ``` 234: 235: ### 2. After Cloning 236: Clean up a cloned repo that has committed bloat: 237: ```bash 238: git clone https://github.com/user/repo 239: cd repo 240: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 241: ``` 242: 243: ### 3. Periodic Maintenance 244: Run monthly to keep repos clean: 245: ```bash 246: # Add to cron or run manually 247: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 248: ``` 249: 250: ### 4. Disk Space Recovery 251: Find and clean the biggest repos: 252: ```bash 253: # Find large directories 254: du -sh ~/projects/* | sort -hr | head -10 255: 256: # Clean the largest 257: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/largest-repo 258: ``` 259: 260: ## Integration with Git 261: 262: ### Pre-commit Hook 263: Add to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`: 264: ```bash 265: #!/bin/bash 266: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" 267: ``` 268: 269: ### Alias 270: Add to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`: 271: ```bash 272: alias cleanup='~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh' 273: 274: # Usage: 275: # cd my-repo 276: # cleanup 277: ``` 278: 279: ## Comparison with Original Script 280: 281: | Feature | Original (PersonalSite) | Universal | 282: |---------|------------------------|-----------| 283: | Target | Single repo | Any directory | 284: | Patterns | Jekyll-specific | Universal | 285: | Usage | `./utils/cleanup-repository.sh` | `script.sh [directory]` | 286: | Customization | Hardcoded | Configurable arrays | 287: | Scanning | Manual checks | Automatic detection | 288: | Portability | Project-specific | Portable | 289: 290: ## Troubleshooting 291: 292: ### "Directory does not exist" 293: ```bash 294: # Check the path 295: ls -la /path/to/repo 296: 297: # Use absolute path 298: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /full/path/to/repo 299: ``` 300: 301: ### "No items found to clean" 302: Repository is already clean! This is normal if: 303: - Already ran the script 304: - Repo has good .gitignore 305: - No bloat present 306: 307: ### Script shows items but doesn't remove 308: Make sure you typed "yes" exactly (case-sensitive): 309: ``` 310: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): yes 311: ``` 312: 313: ### Want to skip confirmation (dangerous) 314: ```bash 315: # Auto-confirm (use with caution!) 316: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 317: ``` 318: 319: ## Advanced Usage 320: 321: ### Dry Run (Preview Only) 322: The script always shows preview first - just answer "no" to confirmation: 323: ``` 324: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): no 325: ``` 326: 327: ### Clean Multiple Repos 328: ```bash 329: #!/bin/bash 330: for repo in ~/projects/*; do 331: if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then 332: echo "Cleaning $repo..." 333: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$repo" 334: fi 335: done 336: ``` 337: 338: ### Selective Cleanup 339: Comment out functions in `main()` to skip categories: 340: ```bash 341: main() { 342: # ... 343: cleanup_venvs 344: cleanup_temp_files 345: # cleanup_output_files # Skip this 346: # cleanup_build_artifacts # Skip this 347: cleanup_redundant_dirs 348: # ... 349: } 350: ``` 351: 352: ## Performance 353: 354: ### Speed 355: - Small repos (<100MB): ~1-2 seconds 356: - Medium repos (500MB): ~5-10 seconds 357: - Large repos (1GB+): ~30-60 seconds 358: 359: ### Resource Usage 360: - Memory: Minimal (<10MB) 361: - CPU: Low (scanning files) 362: - Disk: Frees up space 363: 364: ## Best Practices 365: 366: 1. **Run before committing** - Clean before adding files to git 367: 2. **Review preview** - Always check what will be removed 368: 3. **Update .gitignore** - Add patterns to prevent reintroduction 369: 4. **Backup first** - For unfamiliar repos, backup before running 370: 5. **Test builds** - After cleanup, test that builds still work 371: 372: ## Version History 373: 374: | Version | Date | Changes | 375: |---------|------|---------| 376: | 1.0.0 | 2025-11-17 | Initial universal version from PersonalSite cleanup script | 377: 378: ## Related Scripts 379: 380: - **Original:** `/Users/alyshialedlie/code/PersonalSite/utils/cleanup-repository.sh` 381: - PersonalSite-specific version 382: - More detailed verification 383: - Jekyll-focused 384: 385: - **Universal:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 386: - Works on any repo 387: - Configurable patterns 388: - Portable 389: 390: ## Support 391: 392: For issues or improvements: 393: 1. Review this documentation 394: 2. Check script comments for customization 395: 3. Edit pattern arrays for specific needs 396: 4. 24: resolve(); 25: } 26: }; 27: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.COMPLETED, checkAndResolve); 28: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.FAILED, checkAndResolve); 29: this.worker.on(RETRY_EVENTS.CREATED, cron.ScheduledTask { 39: if (!cron.validate(cronSchedule)) { 40: throw new Error(`Invalid cron schedule: ${cronSchedule}`); 41: } 42: logger.info({ cronSchedule }, `Scheduling ${name}`); 43: const task = cron.schedule(cronSchedule, async () => { 44: logger.info(`Cron triggered - starting ${name}`); 45: try { 46: await runFn(); 47: } catch (error) { 48: logError(logger, error, `Scheduled ${name} failed`); 49: } 50: }); 51: logger.info(`${name} scheduled`); 52: return task; 53: } 54: protected setupDefaultEventListeners( 55: logger: Logger, 56: handlers?: { 57: onCreated?: (job: Job) => Record<string, unknown>; 58: onStarted?: (job: Job) => Record<string, unknown>; 59: onCompleted?: (job: Job) => Record<string, unknown>; 60: onFailed?: (job: Job) => Record<string, unknown>; 61: } 62: ): void { 63: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.CREATED, (job: Job) => { 64: logger.info({ jobId: job.id, ...handlers?.onCreated?.(job) }, 'Job created'); 65: }); 66: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.STARTED, (job: Job) => { 67: logger.info({ jobId: job.id, ...handlers?.onStarted?.(job) }, 'Job started'); 68: }); 69: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.COMPLETED, (job: Job) => { 70: const duration = job.completedAt && job.startedAt 71: ? job.completedAt.getTime() - job.startedAt.getTime() 72: : undefined; 73: logger.info({ jobId: job.id, duration, ...handlers?.onCompleted?.(job) }, 'Job completed'); 74: }); 75: this.worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.FAILED, (job: Job) => { 76: logError(logger, job.error, 'Job failed', { jobId: job.id, ...handlers?.onFailed?.(job) }); 77: }); 78: } 79: getStats(): JobStats { 80: return this.worker.getStats(); 81: } 82: } 1: import argparse 2: import json 3: import math 4: import subprocess 5: import sys 6: from collections import defaultdict 7: from datetime import datetime, "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}" 76: } 77: print_warning() { 78: echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}" 79: } 80: print_error() { 81: echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}" 82: } 83: print_info() { 84: echo -e "${BLUE}→ $1${NC}" 85: } 86: get_size() { 87: local path="$1" 88: if [ -e "$path" ]; then 89: du -sh "$path" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 90: else 91: echo "N/A" 92: fi 93: } 94: count_files() { 95: local path="$1" 96: if [ -d "$path" ]; then 97: find "$path" -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' 98: else 99: echo "0" 100: fi 101: } 102: scan_venvs() { 103: print_info "Scanning for Python virtual environments..." >&2 104: 52: const HOWTO_TYPE = 'HowTo'; 53: const API_REFERENCE_TYPE = 'APIReference'; 54: const SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE = 'SoftwareApplication'; 55: const SOFTWARE_SOURCE_TYPE = 'SoftwareSourceCode'; 56: const TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE = 'TechArticle'; 57: function inferSchemaType(readmePath: string, content: string, context: SchemaContext): string { 58: const pathLower = readmePath.toLowerCase(); 59: const contentLower = content.toLowerCase(); 60: if (pathLower.includes('test') || contentLower.includes('testing guide')) { 61: return HOWTO_TYPE; 62: } 63: if (pathLower.includes('api') || contentLower.includes('api reference') || contentLower.includes('endpoints')) { 64: return API_REFERENCE_TYPE; 65: } 66: if (context.hasPackageJson || context.hasPyproject) { 67: return SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE; 88: } 89: if (hasDescription) { 90: return true; 91: } 92: return false; 93: } 94: function truncateDescription(description: string): string { 95: if (description.length <= DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH) { 96: return description; 138: } 139: if (schemaType === SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE) { 140: schema.applicationCategory = 'DeveloperApplication'; 141: schema.operatingSystem = 'Cross-platform'; 142: } 143: } 144: function addArticleSchemaFields(schema: SchemaObject, schemaType: string): void { 145: if (schemaType !== TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE && schemaType !== HOWTO_TYPE) { 146: return; 174: const warnings: string[] = []; 175: if (!schema['@context']) { 176: errors.push('Missing @context'); 177: } 178: if (!schema['@type']) { 179: errors.push('Missing @type'); 180: } 181: if (!schema.name) { 182: warnings.push('Missing name property'); 183: } 184: if (!schema.description) { 185: warnings.push('Missing description property'); 186: } 187: try { 188: JSON.stringify(schema); 189: } catch (error) { 190: errors.push(`Invalid JSON: ${(error as Error).message}`); 200: if (schema.name) { 201: improvements.push('Added structured name/title'); 202: } 203: if (schema.description) { 204: improvements.push('Added structured description'); 205: } 206: if (schema.codeRepository) { 207: improvements.push('Linked to code repository'); 208: } 209: if (schema.programmingLanguage) { 210: improvements.push('Specified programming languages'); 211: } 212: return improvements; 213: } 214: function collectRichResultsEligibility(schemaType: string): string[] { 215: const richResults: string[] = []; 216: if (schemaType === HOWTO_TYPE) { 217: richResults.push('How-to rich results'); 218: } 219: if (schemaType === SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE) { 220: richResults.push('Software app rich results'); 221: } 222: if (schemaType === TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE) { 223: richResults.push('Article rich results'); 238: } 239: if (score >= SCHEMA_RATING_THRESHOLDS.GOOD_MIN_SCORE) { 240: return 'Good'; 241: } 242: if (score >= SCHEMA_RATING_THRESHOLDS.FAIR_MIN_SCORE) { 243: return 'Fair'; 244: } 245: return 'Needs Improvement'; 246: } 247: function analyzeSchemaImpactData(originalContent: string, enhancedContent: string, schema: SchemaObject): SchemaImpact { 248: const schemaType = schema['@type']; 249: const impact: SchemaImpact = { 250: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 251: schemaType, 252: metrics: { 253: contentSize: { 254: original: originalContent.length, 255: enhanced: enhancedContent.length, 256: increase: enhancedContent.length - originalContent.length 257: }, 258: schemaProperties: Object.keys(schema).length, 259: structuredDataAdded: true 260: }, 261: seoImprovements: collectSeoImprovements(schema), 262: richResultsEligibility: collectRichResultsEligibility(schemaType) 263: }; 146: await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, '01-bugfix-plan.md'), plannerResult.stdout); 147: logStage(logger, '2/5: Running bug-detective plugin'); 148: const detectiveResult = await this.runSlashCommand('/bug-detective:bug-detective', repoPath); 149: results.stages.push({ name: 'bug-detective', status: 'completed', output: detectiveResult.stdout }); 150: await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, '02-bug-detective-report.md'), detectiveResult.stdout); 151: logStage(logger, '3/5: Running audit plugin'); 152: const auditResult = await this.runSlashCommand('/audit:audit', repoPath); 153: results.stages.push({ name: 'audit', status: 'completed', output: auditResult.stdout }); 163: results.stages.push({ name: 'ceo-quality-controller', status: 'completed', output: qualityResult.stdout }); 164: await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, '04-quality-control.md'), qualityResult.stdout); 165: await this.branchManager.commitChanges(repoPath, { 166: message: 'chore(audit): quality control validation complete', 167: description: 'Generated with Claude Code AlephAuto', 168: jobId: job.id, 169: }); 170: logStage(logger, '5/5: Running refractor plugin to implement fixes'); 171: const refractorResult = await this.runSlashCommand('/refractor:refractor', repoPath); 172: results.stages.push({ name: 'refractor', status: 'completed', output: refractorResult.stdout }); Sidequest - Job Queue Framework 2: 3: <script type="application/ld+json"> 4: { 5: "@context": "https://schema.org", 6: "@type": "APIReference", 7: "name": "Sidequest - Job Queue Framework", 8: "description": "The **sidequest** directory contains the core job queue framework for AlephAuto automation pipelines. It provides event-driven job management with SQLite persistence, Sentry integration, and Git wo...", 9: "additionalType": "https://schema.org/TechArticle" 10: } 11: </script> 12: 13: 14: The **sidequest** directory contains the core job queue framework for AlephAuto automation pipelines. It provides event-driven job management with SQLite persistence, Sentry integration, and Git workflow automation. 15: 16: ## Architecture 17: 18: ``` 19: sidequest/ 20: ├── core/ # Base framework components 21: │ ├── server.ts # SidequestServer base class (job queue engine) 22: │ ├── database.ts # SQLite persistence layer 23: │ ├── config.ts # Centralized configuration 24: │ └── index.ts # Module exports 25: │ 26: ├── pipeline-core/ # Business logic for pipelines 27: │ ├── cache/ # Git-aware caching (Redis integration) 28: │ ├── config/ # Repository configuration loader 29: │ ├── errors/ # Error classification & retry logic 30: │ ├── extractors/ # Python code block extraction 31: │ ├── git/ # Branch management, PR creation 32: │ ├── models/ # Python Pydantic models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup) 33: │ ├── reports/ # HTML/JSON/Markdown report generators 34: │ ├── scanners/ # AST pattern detection, repository scanning 35: │ ├── similarity/ # Multi-layer similarity algorithm (Python) 36: │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 37: │ └── utils/ # Error helpers 38: │ 39: ├── pipeline-runners/ # Pipeline entry points 40: │ ├── duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts # 7-stage duplicate detection 41: │ ├── claude-health-pipeline.ts # Claude Code health checks 42: │ ├── git-activity-pipeline.ts # Git activity reports 43: │ ├── gitignore-pipeline.ts # .gitignore management 44: │ ├── repo-cleanup-pipeline.ts # Repository cleanup 45: │ ├── schema-enhancement-pipeline.ts # Schema.org injection 46: │ ├── plugin-management-pipeline.ts # Plugin management 47: │ ├── bugfix-audit-pipeline.ts # Bugfix audit workflow 48: │ ├── dashboard-populate-pipeline.ts # Dashboard data population 49: │ ├── collect_git_activity.py # Git activity collector 50: │ └── test-refactor-pipeline.ts # Test suite refactoring 51: │ 52: ├── workers/ # Worker implementations (extend SidequestServer) 53: │ ├── duplicate-detection-worker.ts # Duplicate detection jobs 54: │ ├── claude-health-worker.ts # Health check jobs 55: │ ├── git-activity-worker.ts # Git activity jobs 56: │ ├── gitignore-worker.ts # .gitignore jobs 57: │ ├── repo-cleanup-worker.ts # Cleanup jobs 58: │ ├── repomix-worker.ts # Repomix jobs 59: │ ├── schema-enhancement-worker.ts # Schema jobs 60: │ ├── bugfix-audit-worker.ts # Bugfix audit jobs 61: │ ├── dashboard-populate-worker.ts # Dashboard populate jobs 62: │ └── test-refactor-worker.ts # Test refactor jobs 63: │ 64: ├── pipeline-core/types/ # TypeScript type definitions 65: │ ├── scan-orchestrator-types.ts # Scan orchestrator interfaces 66: │ ├── migration-types.ts # Migration definitions 67: │ └── duplicate-detection-types.ts # Zod schemas + types 68: │ 69: ├── utils/ # Utility modules 70: │ ├── logger.ts # Pino component logger 71: │ ├── doppler-resilience.ts # Doppler fallback handling 72: │ ├── directory-scanner.ts # Directory scanning 73: │ ├── pipeline-names.ts # Pipeline name constants 74: │ ├── plugin-manager.ts # Plugin management 75: │ ├── report-generator.ts # Report utilities 76: │ ├── gitignore-repomix-updater.ts # Gitignore sync 77: │ ├── schema-mcp-tools.ts # Schema MCP tools 78: │ └── refactor-test-suite.ts # Test refactoring utility 79: │ 80: └── config files 81: ├── .env.example # Environment template 82: ├── .gitignore # Git ignore rules 83: ├── git-report-config.json # Git report config 84: └── repomix.config.json # Repomix configuration 85: ``` 86: 87: ## Core Framework 88: 89: ### SidequestServer (core/server.ts) 90: 91: The base class for all workers with: 92: 93: - **Event-driven lifecycle**: `created → queued → running → completed/failed` 94: - **Concurrency control**: Configurable max concurrent jobs (default: 3) 95: - **SQLite persistence**: Job history stored in `data/jobs.db` 96: - **Sentry integration**: Error tracking and performance monitoring 97: - **Git workflow**: Optional branch creation, commits, and PR automation 98: - **Auto-retry with circuit breaker**: Classifies errors as retryable/non-retryable 99: 100: ```typescript 101: import { SidequestServer } from './core/server.ts'; 102: 103: class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 104: constructor(options) { 105: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-job-type' }); 106: } 107: 108: async runJobHandler(job) { 109: // Implement job logic 110: return { success: true }; 111: } 112: } 113: ``` 114: 115: ### Error Classification (pipeline-core/errors/error-classifier.ts) 116: 117: Intelligent error handling with retry logic: 118: 119: - **Retryable**: ETIMEDOUT, ECONNRESET, 5xx HTTP errors, rate limits 120: - **Non-retryable**: ENOENT, EACCES, 4xx HTTP errors, validation errors 121: - **Suggested delays**: Rate limits (60s), timeouts (10s), connection errors (5s) 122: 123: ## Pipelines 124: 125: ### 1. Duplicate Detection Pipeline 126: 127: 7-stage pipeline combining JavaScript (stages 1-2) and Python (stages 3-7): 128: 129: ```bash 130: doppler run -- RUN_ON_STARTUP=true npx tsx pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts 131: ``` 132: 133: **Stages:** 134: 1. Repository scanning (JS) 135: 2. AST pattern detection with ast-grep (JS) 136: 3. Code block extraction (Python) 137: 4. Semantic annotation (Python) 138: 5. Multi-layer similarity calculation (Python) 139: 6. Duplicate grouping (Python) 140: 7. Report generation (HTML/JSON/Markdown) 141: 142: **Similarity Algorithm Layers:** 143: - Layer 0: Complexity filtering (trivial code exclusion) 144: - Layer 1: Exact matching (hash-based, O(n)) 145: - Layer 2: Structural similarity (AST-based, configurable threshold) 146: - Layer 3: Semantic validation (category, tags, method chains) 147: - Layer 4: Quality filtering (group quality score) 148: 149: ### 2. Claude Health Pipeline 150: 151: Health checks for Claude Code configuration: 152: 153: ```bash 154: npm run claude:health # Immediate check 155: npm run claude:health:detailed # With component details 156: npm run claude:health:schedule # Cron scheduler (daily 8 AM) 157: ``` 158: 159: **Checks:** 160: - Configuration validation (settings.json, skill-rules.json) 161: - Component inventory (skills, agents, hooks, plugins) 162: - Hook permissions and executability 163: - Performance log analysis (slow hooks, failures) 164: - Plugin duplicate detection 165: 166: ### 3. Git Activity Pipeline 167: 168: Weekly/monthly git activity reports: 169: 170: ```bash 171: node --strip-types pipeline-runners/git-activity-pipeline.ts --run --weekly 172: node --strip-types pipeline-runners/git-activity-pipeline.ts --run --monthly 173: python3 pipeline-runners/collect_git_activity.py --weekly 174: ``` 175: 176: **Features:** 177: - Multi-repository scanning 178: - Language distribution analysis 179: - Commit frequency metrics 180: - Jekyll-formatted markdown output 181: 182: **`git-report-config.json` defaults:** 183: - Scans `~/code`, `~/reports`, plus `~/schema-org-file-system`, `~/claude-tool-use`, and `~/dotfiles` 184: - Includes dotfiles, uses max depth `2`, and excludes common build/dependency folders (`node_modules`, `.git`, `dist`, `build`, etc.) 185: - Report schedules: weekly `Sunday 20:00`, monthly `1st of month 08:00`, quarterly disabled 186: - Outputs reports into `~/code/PersonalSite/_work` with SVG visualizations under `assets/images/git-activity-{year}` 187: - Weekly and monthly report generation are enabled; both default to markdown output and no auto-commit/push 188: 189: ### 4. Repository Cleanup Pipeline 190: 191: Automated cleanup of build artifacts and virtual environments: 192: 193: ```bash 194: npm run cleanup:dryrun # Preview changes 195: npm run cleanup:once # Execute cleanup 196: ``` 197: 198: **Targets:** 199: - Python venvs (.venv, __pycache__, *.pyc) 200: - Node modules (node_modules, .npm-cache) 201: - Build artifacts (dist, build, *.log) 202: - Temporary files (temp, tmp, *.swp) 203: 204: ### 5. Schema Enhancement Pipeline 205: 206: Inject Schema.org structured data into HTML documents. 207: 208: ### 6. GitIgnore Pipeline 209: 210: Sync and validate .gitignore files across repositories. 211: 212: ## Python Components 213: 214: Located in `pipeline-core/` with dependencies on: 215: - **Pydantic**: Data models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup, ScanReport) 216: - **Standard library**: hashlib, re, json, sys 217: 218: ### Models (pipeline-core/models/) 219: 220: ```python 221: from code_block import CodeBlock, SourceLocation 222: from duplicate_group import DuplicateGroup 223: from consolidation_suggestion import ConsolidationSuggestion 224: from scan_report import ScanReport, ScanMetrics 225: ``` 226: 227: ### Similarity Module (pipeline-core/similarity/) 228: 229: ```python 230: from similarity.structural import calculate_structural_similarity, normalize_code 231: from similarity.grouping import group_by_similarity 232: from similarity.semantic import are_semantically_compatible, validate_duplicate_group 233: ``` 234: 235: **Configuration** (via environment variables): 236: - `STRUCTURAL_THRESHOLD`: Minimum similarity (default: 0.90) 237: - `MIN_GROUP_QUALITY`: Group quality threshold (default: 0.70) 238: - `MIN_LINE_COUNT`: Minimum lines (default: 1) 239: - `MIN_UNIQUE_TOKENS`: Minimum tokens (default: 3) 240: 241: ## Configuration 242: 243: ### Environment Variables 244: 245: Managed via Doppler (`bottleneck` project): 246: 247: ```bash 248: doppler setup --project bottleneck --config dev 249: doppler run -- npx tsx pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts 250: ``` 251: 252: **Key variables:** 253: - `JOBS_API_PORT`: API server port (default: 8080) 254: - `SENTRY_DSN`: Sentry error tracking 255: - `ENABLE_GIT_WORKFLOW`: Enable branch/PR creation 256: - `ENABLE_PR_CREATION`: Auto-create PRs for changes 257: - `PIPELINE_DEBUG`: Enable verbose Python debug output 258: 259: ### Repository Configuration (config/scan-repositories.json) 260: 261: ```json 262: { 263: "repositories": [ 264: { 265: "name": "my-repo", 266: "path": "/path/to/repo", 267: "enabled": true, 268: "priority": "high", 269: "scanFrequency": "daily" 270: } 271: ], 272: "groups": [ 273: { 274: "name": "web-projects", 275: "repositories": ["repo1", "repo2"], 276: "scanType": "inter-project" 277: } 278: ] 279: } 280: ``` 281: 282: ## Reports 283: 284: Generated in multiple formats: 285: 286: ### HTML Reports 287: Interactive dashboards with: 288: - Metrics cards (code blocks, duplicate groups, LOC reduction) 289: - Distribution charts (by strategy, complexity) 290: - Duplicate group details with impact scores 291: - Consolidation suggestions with ROI scores 292: 293: ### JSON Reports 294: Machine-readable output for integration: 295: ```json 296: { 297: "scan_type": "intra-project", 298: "scan_metadata": { ... }, 299: "metrics": { ... }, 300: "duplicate_groups": [ ... ], 301: "suggestions": [ ... ] 302: } 303: ``` 304: 305: ### Markdown Reports 306: Human-readable summaries for documentation. 307: 308: ## Development 309: 310: ### Adding a New Worker 311: 312: 1. Create worker in `workers/`: 313: ```typescript 314: import { SidequestServer } from '../core/server.ts'; 315: 316: export class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 317: constructor(options = {}) { 318: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-worker' }); 319: } 320: 321: async runJobHandler(job) { 322: // Job implementation 323: return { status: 'completed' }; 324: } 325: } 326: ``` 327: 328: 2. Create pipeline in `pipeline-runners/`: 329: ```typescript 330: import { MyWorker } from '../workers/my-worker.ts'; 331: 332: const worker = new MyWorker({ maxConcurrent: 1 }); 333: worker.addJob({ data: 'example' }); 334: ``` 335: 336: ### Type Definitions 337: 338: Use Zod schemas with TypeScript inference: 339: ```typescript 340: import { z } from 'zod'; 341: 342: export const MyJobDataSchema = z.object({ 343: type: z.literal('my-job'), 344: target: z.string() 345: }).strict(); 346: 347: export type MyJobData = z.infer<typeof MyJobDataSchema>; 348: ``` 349: 350: ## Testing 351: 352: ```bash 353: npm test # Unit tests 354: npm run test:integration # Integration tests 355: npm run typecheck # TypeScript checks 356: ``` 357: 358: ## Related Documentation 359: 360: - `../docs/API_REFERENCE.md` - REST API endpoints 361: - `../docs/architecture/ERROR_HANDLING.md` - Retry logic details 362: - `../docs/architecture/TYPE_SYSTEM.md` - Type patterns 363: - Job, type JobStats } from './base-pipeline.ts'; 7: import fs from 'fs/promises'; 8: import path from 'path'; 9: import { isDirectExecution } from '../utils/execution-helpers.ts'; 10: const logger = createComponentLogger('BugfixAuditPipeline'); 11: interface BugfixAuditOptions { 12: maxConcurrent?: number; 13: activeDocsDir?: string; 14: outputBaseDir?: string; 15: gitBaseBranch?: string; 16: gitBranchPrefix?: string; 17: gitDryRun?: boolean; 18: [key: string]: unknown; 19: } 20: interface JobData { 21: projectName: string; 22: markdownFile: string; 23: repoPath: string; 24: } 25: interface JobResult { 26: pullRequestUrl?: string; 27: } 28: interface RunResult { 29: status: string; 30: jobsCreated?: number; 31: duration?: number; 32: active?: number; 33: queued?: number; 34: total?: number; 35: completed?: number; 36: failed?: number; 37: } 38: class BugfixAuditPipeline extends BasePipeline<BugfixAuditWorker> { 39: constructor(options: BugfixAuditOptions = {}) { 40: super(new BugfixAuditWorker({ 41: ...options, 42: maxConcurrent: options.maxConcurrent ?? 3, 43: logDir: config.logDir, 44: sentryDsn: config.sentryDsn, 45: activeDocsDir: options.activeDocsDir ?? path.join(config.homeDir, 'dev', 'active'), 46: outputBaseDir: options.outputBaseDir ?? path.join(config.homeDir, 'code', 'jobs', 'sidequest', 'bug-fixes', 'output'), 47: gitBaseBranch: options.gitBaseBranch ?? config.gitBaseBranch, 48: gitBranchPrefix: options.gitBranchPrefix ?? 'bugfix', 49: gitDryRun: options.gitDryRun ?? config.gitDryRun, 50: })); 51: this.setupDefaultEventListeners(logger, { 52: onCreated: (job) => { 53: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 54: return { projectName: data.projectName, markdownFile: data.markdownFile }; 55: }, 56: onStarted: (job) => ({ projectName: (job.data as unknown as JobData).projectName }), 57: onCompleted: (job) => { 58: const result = job.result as unknown as JobResult | undefined; 59: return { 60: projectName: (job.data as unknown as JobData).projectName, 61: pullRequestUrl: result?.pullRequestUrl, 62: }; 63: }, 64: onFailed: (job) => ({ projectName: (job.data as unknown as JobData).projectName }), 65: }); 66: } 67: async runBugfixAudit(): Promise<RunResult> { 68: logStart(logger, 'bugfix audit pipeline', { timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }); 69: const startTime = Date.now(); 70: const jobs = await this.worker.createJobsForAllMarkdownFiles(); 71: if (jobs.length === 0) { 72: logger.info('No eligible projects found for bug fix audit'); 73: return { status: 'completed', jobsCreated: 0 }; 74: } 75: logger.info({ jobCount: jobs.length }, 'Jobs created, waiting for completion'); 76: await this.waitForCompletion(); 77: const duration = Date.now() - startTime; 78: const stats = this.getStats(); 79: logger.info({ 80: durationSeconds: Math.round(duration / TIME_MS.SECOND), 81: totalJobs: stats.total, 82: completed: stats.completed, 83: failed: stats.failed, 84: }, 'Bugfix audit complete'); 85: await this.saveRunSummary(stats, duration, jobs); 86: return { status: 'completed', duration, ...stats }; 87: } 88: private async saveRunSummary(stats: JobStats, duration: number, jobs: Job[]): Promise<void> { 89: const summary = { 90: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 91: duration, 92: stats, 93: jobs: jobs.map((job) => { 94: const data = job.data as unknown as JobData; 95: const result = job.result as unknown as JobResult | undefined; 96: return { 97: id: job.id, 98: projectName: data.projectName, 99: markdownFile: data.markdownFile, 100: repoPath: data.repoPath, 101: status: job.status, 102: pullRequestUrl: result?.pullRequestUrl, 103: }; 104: }), 105: }; 106: const logsDir = path.join(config.homeDir, 'code', 'jobs', 'sidequest', 'bug-fixes', 'logs'); 107: await fs.mkdir(logsDir, { recursive: true }); 108: const summaryPath = path.join(logsDir, `run-summary-${Date.now()}.json`); 109: await fs.writeFile(summaryPath, JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)); 110: logger.info({ summaryPath }, 'Run summary saved'); 111: } 112: scheduleAudits(cronSchedule: string = '0 1 * * *') { 113: return this.scheduleCron(logger, 'bugfix audit', cronSchedule, () => this.runBugfixAudit()); 114: } 115: scheduleTonight(): void { 116: const now = new Date(); 117: const tonight1AM = new Date(); 118: tonight1AM.setHours(1, 0, 0, 0); 119: if (now.getHours() >= 1) { 120: tonight1AM.setDate(tonight1AM.getDate() + 1); 121: } 122: const delay = tonight1AM.getTime() - now.getTime(); 123: logger.info({ 124: scheduledTime: tonight1AM.toISOString(), 125: delayMs: delay, 126: }, 'One-time job scheduled for tonight at 1 AM'); 127: setTimeout(async () => { 128: logger.info('One-time job triggered'); 129: try { 130: await this.runBugfixAudit(); 131: } catch (error) { 132: logError(logger, error, 'One-time bugfix audit failed'); 133: } 134: }, delay); 135: } 136: } 137: if (isDirectExecution(import.meta.url)) { 138: const args = process.argv.slice(2); 139: const runNow = args.includes('--run-now') || args.includes('--now'); 140: const once = args.includes('--once') || args.includes('--one-time'); 141: const recurring = args.includes('--recurring') || args.includes('--cron'); 142: let schedule: string | null = null; 143: const scheduleIndex = args.indexOf('--schedule'); 144: if (scheduleIndex !== -1 && args[scheduleIndex + 1]) { 145: schedule = args[scheduleIndex + 1]; 146: } 147: const pipeline = new BugfixAuditPipeline(); 148: if (runNow) { 149: logger.info('Running bugfix audit immediately'); 150: pipeline.runBugfixAudit() 151: .then((result) => { 152: logger.info({ result }, 'Bugfix audit completed'); 153: process.exit(0); 154: }) 155: .catch((error: unknown) => { 156: logError(logger, error, 'Bugfix audit failed'); 157: process.exit(1); 158: }); 159: } else if (recurring) { 160: const cronSchedule = schedule ?? '0 1 * * *'; 161: logStart(logger, 'bugfix audit pipeline in scheduled mode', { cronSchedule }); 162: pipeline.scheduleAudits(cronSchedule); 163: logger.info('Bugfix audit pipeline running. Press Ctrl+C to stop.'); 164: } else if (once) { 165: pipeline.scheduleTonight(); 166: logger.info('One-time execution scheduled. Process will keep running.'); 167: } else { 168: pipeline.scheduleTonight(); 169: logger.info('No flags specified — defaulting to one-time tonight at 1 AM.'); 170: } 171: } 172: export { BugfixAuditPipeline }; 1: #!/usr/bin/env -S node --strip-types 2: import { ClaudeHealthWorker } from '../workers/claude-health-worker.ts'; 3: import { createComponentLogger, logError, logStart } from '../utils/logger.ts'; 4: import { config } from '../core/config.ts'; 5: import { BYTES_PER_KB, MAX_SCORE } from '../core/constants.ts'; 6: import { HEALTH_SCORE_THRESHOLDS } from '../core/score-thresholds.ts'; 7: import { BasePipeline, type JobStats } from './base-pipeline.ts'; 8: import { isDirectExecution } from '../utils/execution-helpers.ts'; 9: const logger = createComponentLogger('ClaudeHealthPipeline'); 10: interface HealthCheckOptions { 11: detailed?: boolean; 12: validateConfig?: boolean; 13: checkPerformance?: boolean; 14: analyzePlugins?: boolean; 15: [key: string]: unknown; 16: } 17: interface PipelineOptions { 18: detailed: boolean; 19: skipValidation: boolean; 20: skipPerformance: boolean; 21: skipPlugins: boolean; 22: } 23: interface Recommendation { 24: priority: string; 25: type: string; 26: message: string; 27: action: string; 28: details?: Array<{ 29: category?: string; 30: plugins?: string[]; 31: suggestion?: string; 32: }>; 33: } 34: interface HealthCheckResult { 35: summary: { 36: healthScore: number; 37: status: string; 38: message: string; 39: criticalIssues: number; 40: warnings: number; 41: }; 42: checks: { 43: components?: { 44: skills: number; 45: agents: number; 46: commands: number; 47: activeTasks: number; 48: archivedTasks: number; 49: }; 50: hooks?: { 51: executableHooks: number; 52: totalHooks: number; 53: registeredHooks: number; 54: hooks?: Array<{ name: string; executable: boolean }>; 55: }; 56: plugins?: { 57: totalPlugins: number; 58: duplicateCategories?: Array<{ 59: category: string; 60: count: number; 61: plugins: string[]; 62: }>; 63: }; 64: environment?: { 65: nodeVersion?: string; 66: npmVersion?: string; 67: direnv?: boolean; 68: direnvAllowed?: boolean; 69: }; 70: configuration?: { 71: settingsJson: { valid: boolean }; 72: skillRulesJson: { valid: boolean }; 73: packageJson: { valid: boolean }; 74: envrc: { exists: boolean }; 75: }; 76: performance?: { 77: logExists: boolean; 78: logSize: number; 79: totalEntries: number; 80: slowHooks: number; 81: failures: number; 82: slowHookDetails?: Array<{ hook: string; duration: number }>; 83: }; 84: }; 85: recommendations: Recommendation[]; 86: duration: number; 87: } 88: class ClaudeHealthPipeline extends BasePipeline<ClaudeHealthWorker> { 89: private options: PipelineOptions; 90: constructor(options: Record<string, unknown> = {}) { 91: super(new ClaudeHealthWorker({ 92: maxConcurrent: 1, 93: logDir: config.logDir, 94: sentryDsn: config.sentryDsn, 95: ...options 96: })); 97: this.options = { 98: detailed: process.env.DETAILED === 'true', 99: skipValidation: process.env.SKIP_VALIDATION === 'true', 100: skipPerformance: process.env.SKIP_PERFORMANCE === 'true', 101: skipPlugins: process.env.SKIP_PLUGINS === 'true' 102: }; 103: this.setupDefaultEventListeners(logger, { 104: onCompleted: (job) => { 105: const result = job.result as unknown as HealthCheckResult; 106: this.displayResults(result); 107: return { 108: healthScore: result.summary.healthScore, 109: status: result.summary.status, 110: criticalIssues: result.summary.criticalIssues, 111: warnings: result.summary.warnings, 112: }; 113: }, 114: }); 115: } 116: private displayResults(result: HealthCheckResult): void { 117: printSummary(result); 118: if (result.recommendations.length > 0) { 119: printRecommendations(result.recommendations); 120: } 121: if (this.options.detailed) { 122: printDetailedChecks(result.checks); 123: } 124: } 125: async runHealthCheck(options: HealthCheckOptions = {}): Promise<JobStats> { 126: logStart(logger, 'health check', { options }); 127: const startTime = Date.now(); 128: try { 129: const job = this.worker.addJob({ 130: detailed: options.detailed ?? this.options.detailed, 131: validateConfig: options.validateConfig ?? !this.options.skipValidation, 132: checkPerformance: options.checkPerformance ?? !this.options.skipPerformance, 133: analyzePlugins: options.analyzePlugins ?? !this.options.skipPlugins, 134: }); 135: logger.info({ jobId: job.id }, 'Health check job created'); 136: await this.waitForCompletion(); 137: const duration = Date.now() - startTime; 138: const stats = this.getStats(); 139: logger.info({ 140: duration, 141: stats 142: }, 'Health check pipeline completed'); 143: return stats; 144: } catch (error) { 145: logError(logger, error, 'Health check pipeline failed'); 146: throw error; 147: } 148: } 149: scheduleHealthChecks(cronSchedule: string) { 150: return this.scheduleCron(logger, 'health check', cronSchedule, () => this.runHealthCheck()); 151: } 152: } 153: function printSummary(result: HealthCheckResult): void { 154: logger.info('\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗'); 155: logger.info('║ Claude Code Health Check Summary ║'); 156: logger.info('╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n'); 157: logger.info( 158: `Health Score: ${getScoreColor(result.summary.healthScore)}${result.summary.healthScore}/${MAX_SCORE}\x1b[0m` 159: ); 160: logger.info(`Status: ${result.summary.message}\n`); 161: if (result.checks.components) { 162: logger.info('Component Inventory:'); 163: logger.info(` Skills: ${result.checks.components.skills}`); 164: logger.info(` Agents: ${result.checks.components.agents}`); 165: logger.info(` Commands: ${result.checks.components.commands}`); 166: logger.info(` Hooks: ${result.checks.hooks?.executableHooks ?? 0}/${result.checks.hooks?.totalHooks ?? 0} executable`); 167: logger.info(` Registered: ${result.checks.hooks?.registeredHooks ?? 0} hook types`); 168: if (result.checks.plugins) { 169: logger.info(` Plugins: ${result.checks.plugins.totalPlugins}`); 170: } 171: logger.info(` Active Tasks: ${result.checks.components.activeTasks}`); 172: logger.info(` Archived: ${result.checks.components.archivedTasks}`); 173: logger.info(''); 174: } 175: // Environment 176: if (result.checks.environment) { 177: const env = result.checks.environment; 178: logger.info('Environment:'); 179: logger.info(` Node.js: ${env.nodeVersion ?? 'not found'}`); 180: logger.info(` npm: ${env.npmVersion ?? 'not found'}`); 181: logger.info(` direnv: ${env.direnv ? '✓ installed' : '✗ not installed'}`); 182: if (env.direnv && !env.direnvAllowed) { 183: logger.info(` \x1b[33m⚠ Environment variables not loaded\x1b[0m`); 184: } 185: logger.info(''); 186: } 187: // Summary statistics 188: logger.info('Summary:'); 189: logger.info(` Critical Issues: ${result.summary.criticalIssues}`); 190: logger.info(` Warnings: ${result.summary.warnings}`); 191: logger.info(` Duration: ${result.duration}ms\n`); 192: } 193: /** 194: * printRecommendations. 195: */ 196: function printRecommendations(recommendations: Recommendation[]): void { 197: logger.info('╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗'); 198: logger.info('║ Recommendations ║'); 199: logger.info('╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n'); 200: for (const rec of recommendations) { 201: const icon = rec.priority === 'high' ? '🔴' : rec.priority === 'medium' ? '🟡' : '✅'; 202: const priority = rec.priority.toUpperCase(); 203: logger.info(`${icon} [${priority}] ${rec.type}`); 204: logger.info(` ${rec.message}`); 205: logger.info(` Action: ${rec.action}`); 206: if (rec.details) { 207: logger.info(' Details:'); 208: for (const detail of rec.details) { 209: if (detail.category && detail.plugins) { 210: logger.info(` • ${detail.category}: ${detail.plugins.join(', ')}`); 211: if (detail.suggestion) { 212: logger.info(` → ${detail.suggestion}`); 213: } 214: } 215: } 216: } 217: logger.info(''); 218: } 219: } 220: /** 221: * printDetailedChecks. 222: */ 223: function printDetailedChecks(checks: HealthCheckResult['checks']): void { 224: logger.info('╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗'); 225: logger.info('║ Detailed Check Results ║'); 226: logger.info('╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n'); 227: if (checks.configuration) { 228: logger.info('Configuration Files:'); 229: logger.info(` settings.json: ${checks.configuration.settingsJson.valid ? '✓' : '✗'} valid`); 230: logger.info(` skill-rules.json: ${checks.configuration.skillRulesJson.valid ? '✓' : '✗'} valid`); 231: logger.info(` package.json: ${checks.configuration.packageJson.valid ? '✓' : '✗'} valid`); 232: logger.info(` .envrc: ${checks.configuration.envrc.exists ? '✓' : '✗'} exists`); 233: logger.info(''); 234: } 235: // Hooks 236: if (checks.hooks?.hooks) { 237: logger.info('Hook Details:'); 238: for (const hook of checks.hooks.hooks) { 239: const status = hook.executable ? '✓' : '✗'; 240: logger.info(` ${status} ${hook.name}`); 241: } 242: logger.info(''); 243: } 244: // Plugins 245: if (checks.plugins?.duplicateCategories && checks.plugins.duplicateCategories.length > 0) { 246: logger.info('Duplicate Plugin Categories:'); 247: for (const cat of checks.plugins.duplicateCategories) { 248: logger.info(` ${cat.category} (${cat.count} plugins):`); 249: for (const plugin of cat.plugins) { 250: logger.info(` • ${plugin}`); 251: } 252: } 253: logger.info(''); 254: } 255: // Performance 256: if (checks.performance?.logExists) { 257: logger.info('Performance:'); 258: logger.info(` Log size: ${formatBytes(checks.performance.logSize)}`); 259: logger.info(` Total entries: ${checks.performance.totalEntries}`); 260: logger.info(` Slow hooks: ${checks.performance.slowHooks}`); 261: logger.info(` Failures: ${checks.performance.failures}`); 262: if (checks.performance.slowHookDetails && checks.performance.slowHookDetails.length > 0) { 263: logger.info('\n Slowest hooks:'); 264: for (const hook of checks.performance.slowHookDetails) { 265: logger.info(` • ${hook.hook}: ${hook.duration}ms`); 266: } 267: } 268: logger.info(''); 269: } 270: } 271: /** 272: * getScoreColor. 273: */ 274: function getScoreColor(score: number): string { 275: if (score >= HEALTH_SCORE_THRESHOLDS.HEALTHY_MIN_SCORE) return '\x1b[32m'; 276: if (score >= HEALTH_SCORE_THRESHOLDS.WARNING_MIN_SCORE) return '\x1b[33m'; 277: return '\x1b[31m'; 278: } 279: function formatBytes(bytes: number): string { 280: if (bytes === 0) return '0 Bytes'; 281: const k = BYTES_PER_KB; 282: const sizes = ['Bytes', 'KB', 'MB', 'GB']; 283: const i = Math.floor(Math.log(bytes) / Math.log(k)); 284: return Math.round(bytes / Math.pow(k, i) * 100) / 100 + ' ' + sizes[i]; 285: } 286: async function runCli(): Promise<void> { 287: const pipeline = new ClaudeHealthPipeline(); 288: const cronSchedule = process.env.CLAUDE_HEALTH_CRON_SCHEDULE || '0 8 * * *'; 289: const runOnStartup = config.runOnStartup; recommendations?: unknown[] } | null; 56: logger.info({ 57: jobId: job.id, 58: project: job.data['repository'], 59: filesGenerated: result?.generatedFiles?.length ?? 0, 60: recommendations: result?.recommendations?.length ?? 0 61: }, 'Job completed'); 62: }); 63: worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.FAILED, (job: BaseJob, error: Error) => { 64: logger.error({ 65: jobId: job.id, 66: project: job.data['repository'], 67: error: error.message 68: }, 'Job failed'); 69: }); 70: try { 71: if (targetPath) { 72: const resolvedPath = path.resolve(targetPath); 73: worker.queueProject(resolvedPath); 74: } else { 75: const scanner = new DirectoryScanner({ 76: baseDir: CODE_BASE_DIR, 77: maxDepth: 2, 78: excludeDirs: [ 79: 'node_modules', 80: '.git', 81: 'dist', 82: 'build', 83: 'coverage', 84: '__pycache__', 85: '.venv', 86: 'venv' 87: ] 88: }); 89: const directories = await scanner.scanDirectories(); 90: for (const dir of directories) { 91: const hasTests = await hasTestDirectory(dir.fullPath); 92: if (hasTests) { 93: worker.queueProject(dir.fullPath); 94: } 95: } 96: logger.info({ 97: totalDirectories: directories.length, 98: queuedJobs: worker.queue.length 99: }, 'Scan complete, jobs queued'); 100: } 101: await waitForCompletion(worker); 102: const metrics = worker.getMetrics(); 103: const stats = worker.getStats(); 104: logger.info({ 105: ...metrics, 106: ...stats 107: }, 'Pipeline completed'); 108: return { metrics, stats }; 109: } catch (error) { 110: logger.error({ err: error }, 'Pipeline failed'); 111: throw error; 112: } 113: } 114: async function hasTestDirectory(dirPath: string): Promise<boolean> { 115: const { glob } = await import('glob'); 116: const testFiles = await glob('**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}', { 117: cwd: dirPath, 118: ignore: ['**/node_modules/**'], 119: nodir: true 120: }); 121: return testFiles.length > 0; 122: } 123: function waitForCompletion(worker: TestRefactorWorker): Promise<void> { 124: return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { 125: const deadline = setTimeout(() => { 126: cleanup(); 127: reject(new Error(`waitForCompletion timed out after ${TIMEOUTS.ONE_HOUR_MS}ms`)); 128: }, TIMEOUTS.ONE_HOUR_MS); 129: const cleanup = () => { 130: worker.off(JOB_EVENTS.COMPLETED, checkAndResolve); 131: worker.off(JOB_EVENTS.FAILED, checkAndResolve); 132: worker.off(RETRY_EVENTS.CREATED, checkAndResolve); 133: }; 134: const checkAndResolve = () => { 135: const stats = worker.getStats(); 136: if (stats.active === 0 && stats.queued === 0 && stats.pendingRetries === 0) { 137: clearTimeout(deadline); 138: cleanup(); 139: resolve(); 140: } 141: }; 142: worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.COMPLETED, checkAndResolve); 143: worker.on(JOB_EVENTS.FAILED, checkAndResolve); 144: worker.on(RETRY_EVENTS.CREATED, checkAndResolve); 145: checkAndResolve(); 146: }); 147: } 148: async function main(): Promise<void> { 149: const targetPath = process.argv[2]; 150: if (targetPath) { 151: await runPipeline(targetPath); 152: } else if (RUN_ON_STARTUP) { 153: await runPipeline(); 154: } 155: if (!targetPath) { 156: logger.info({ schedule: CRON_SCHEDULE }, 'Scheduling cron job'); 157: cron.schedule(CRON_SCHEDULE, async () => { 158: logger.info('Running scheduled test refactor pipeline'); 159: await runPipeline(); 160: }); 161: process.on('SIGINT', () => { 162: logger.info('Shutting down'); 163: process.exit(0); 164: }); 165: } 166: } 167: if (isDirectExecution(import.meta.url)) { 168: main().catch(error => { 169: logger.error({ err: error }, 'Fatal error'); 170: process.exit(1); 171: }); 172: } 173: export { runPipeline }; 1: import { realpathSync } from 'fs'; 2: import path from 'path'; 3: import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'; 4: export function isDirectExecution(importMetaUrl: string): boolean { 5: const currentModulePath = fileURLToPath(importMetaUrl); 6: const entryPath = process.argv[1] || process.env.pm_exec_path; 7: if (!entryPath) return false; 8: try { 9: return realpathSync(path.resolve(entryPath)) === realpathSync(currentModulePath); 10: } catch { 11: return false; 12: } 13: } 1: import { TIME_MS } from '../core/units.ts'; 2: export const DURATION_UNKNOWN_LABEL = 'unknown'; 3: export function toISOString(val: Date | string | null | undefined): string | null { 4: if (!val) return null; 5: if (val instanceof Date) return val.toISOString(); 6: return val; 7: } 8: export function calculateDurationSeconds(startTime: Date | string | null, endTime: Date | string | null): number | null { 9: if (!startTime || !endTime) return null; 10: const start = startTime instanceof Date ? startTime : new Date(startTime); 11: const end = endTime instanceof Date ? endTime : new Date(endTime); 12: if (isNaN(start.getTime()) || isNaN(end.getTime())) return null; 13: return Math.round((end.getTime() - start.getTime()) / TIME_MS.SECOND); 14: } 15: export function formatDuration(seconds: number | null | undefined): string { 16: if (seconds === null || seconds === undefined) return DURATION_UNKNOWN_LABEL; 17: if (seconds < TIME_MS.MINUTE / TIME_MS.SECOND) return `${seconds}s`; 18: const hours = Math.floor(seconds / (TIME_MS.HOUR / TIME_MS.SECOND)); 19: const minutes = Math.floor((seconds % (TIME_MS.HOUR / TIME_MS.SECOND)) / (TIME_MS.MINUTE / TIME_MS.SECOND)); 20: const secs = seconds % (TIME_MS.MINUTE / TIME_MS.SECOND); 21: if (hours > 0) { 22: return `${hours}h ${minutes}m ${secs}s`; 23: } 24: return `${minutes}m ${secs}s`; 25: }
repomix.instruction:
# Repomix Repository Analysis Instructions
This repository bundle is generated by Repomix for AI-assisted code review and architecture analysis.
## Bundle characteristics
- Output format: XML - Compression enabled - Comments and empty lines removed - Directory structure and summaries included for navigation - Docs are intentionally excluded to reduce token bloat - Git diffs excluded; git logs disabled/minimized
Treat this bundle as read-only context. Prefer minimal, targeted fixes.
## What to focus on first
Prioritize executable/runtime code and entrypoints:
1. `api/` — backend routes, middleware, websockets, schemas, API utilities 2. `sidequest/` — pipelines, orchestrators, scanners, workers, report generation 3. `frontend/` — UI components, websocket hooks, API client, state/store 4. `scripts/` — automation and operational tooling 5. `config/` — runtime and scanning configuration
## Navigation strategy
1. Start with directory structure + top files list. 2. Identify entrypoints (servers, orchestrators, workers). 3. Follow imports/dependency roots outward. 4. Inspect error-handling, retries/timeouts, and async coordination. 5. Keep recommendations small, localized, and concrete.
## What to ignore
- Large documentation trees / archives - Generated artifacts (including previous repomix outputs) - Media/binaries and large datasets
